Chapter 1 Chapter Notes Frosh = College freshmen or first years Residence = on campus student housing / dorms RA = Resident advisor, aka an older student who lives in residence and helps keep the frosh alive AND real quick note about Hockey - if you're planning to go to college before playing pro-hockey, you usually (not always, but usually) get drafted before you start school. I'm pointing this out just because I know with other sports it's the opposite, you get drafted at the end of college See the end of the chapter for more notes During one of the many therapy sessions that Regulusís parents pay an exorbitant amount of money for, hoping theyíll make him into the charismatic son they wantîlike some kind of psychological boob jobîhis therapist asks him what he would say to his inner child. Being all of seventeen, Regulus thinks itís a bit presumptuous to assume that there is anything inner about his child, but he doesnít bother pointing that out. He has heard enough times, from enough overpaid professionals, that his sarcasm is a defence mechanism. He thought that was obvious, but for whatever reason, people continue to feel the need to point it out. In any case, Regulus doesnít understand the question. ØMy inner child?Æ ØYes,Æ the woman goes on in an infuriatingly calm voice. ØDo you think you would be able to be this cruel to yourselfîØ ØIím not cruel,Æ he isnít. Heís just honest. If that honesty happens to be a little mean spirited thatís hardly something to make a fuss about. His therapist gives him a condescending smile. ØDo you think you would be able to be this hard on yourself,Æ she corrects, Øif you were talking to him?Æ Regulus blinks. ØHim?Æ ØYes.Æ ØMy inner child?Æ ØYes.Æ Regulus lets a few more seconds pass, hoping the woman in front of him will suddenly burst out laughing and confess that this is a joke. She does not. ØDo people usually say no?Æ he asks finally. He swears he sees the flicker of a genuine smile in the corner of her mouth. ØWhat people usually do isnít important, this is about you.Æ Oh joy. ØI donít understand why how old I am makes a difference,Æ Regulus says eventually, feeling, to his dismay, genuine frustration. ØI didnít like myself when I was younger anymore than I like myself now.Æ That actually makes her look kind of sad, which Regulus thinks is unnecessary. Heís lived a very privileged life all things considered. He doesnít think a little self-hatred is anything to get upset about. ØYou donít feel the need to be kind to him? Protect him? Perhaps the way you wish that other people would have?Æ Regulus struggles not to roll his eyes. ØNo.Æ She tilts her head to the side. ØAnd why not?Æ And then, in an embarrassing moment of honesty, Regulus finds himself blurting out; ØBecause heís inadequate.Æ He instantly wants to take it back, but of course he canít. Heís usually much better at not letting things like that slip out. His therapist has that sad look in her eyes again that Regulus is almost certain is breaking some kind of professional code of conduct. Surely therapists should be required to remain neutral at all times. Perhaps heíll write a letter of complaint to the ethics board. ØBy whose standards?Æ she asks eventually, stare intense. ØYours or theirs?Æ Regulusís teeth grind together. He doesnít like where this is going at all. ØTheirs?Æ ØYour parents. Your brother. Was it your own expectations you fell short of, or was it theirs?Æ His eyes narrow. ØWhatís the difference?Æ ØThe difference,Æ she says, in that same infuriatingly soft tone. ØIs that you donít seem to think that you matter in all of this. The way others perceive you determines your entire relationship with yourself. You think your parents and your brother feel that youíve failed to live up to some sort of standard theyíve set for you and therefore you are inadequate. But what about you Regulus? What do you want? What do you need? Have you ever gotten it? Werenít they also inadequate, in some way?Æ Regulus sits stock still, his whole body tense. A long moment of silence passing in which only the ticking of the clock and the distant noises of construction can be heard. ØI think,Æ Regulus says finally, voice quiet and tightly held, Øthat our time is up.Æ Several months later, as he drives away from his hometown, after stiff goodbyes on the front lawnîhis mother squeezing his shoulder and whispering, Ødonít let me downÆ like itís a threatîthe memory of that therapy session comes back to him. Not because there was anything necessarily revelatory about it. But because, watching his childhood home shrink in the rearview mirror doesnít do anything to loosen the tightness in his chest. Because even when the miles rack up and the distance between him and his mother grows, he can still feel the weight of her grip on his shoulder. Heíd thought there would be some satisfaction in finally driving away. That he would be able to leave some of this behindîthe guilt, the insecurity, the aching loneliness. That his motherís voice in his head would dim. But, of course, he was wrong. Because when all is said and done, he canít drive away from himself. Canít escape himself. Or the fact, that he is still, after all this time, inadequate. ØFuck,Æ the word punches out of Regulus as his chest slams into the dashboard. ØSorry, sorry, sorry,Æ Cerci says, ØI didnít see it!Æ ØThe stoplight?Æ Regulus rubs his sternum. ØThe big flashing lights in front of us? You didnít see them? Theyíre multi-coloured.Æ Cerci sighs dramatically, swinging her head in Regulusís direction. Her glaring is somewhat undermined by the pink, heart-shaped sunglasses taking up most of her face. ØYou get bitchy when youíre nervous, anyone ever tell you that?Æ ØYeah, you.Æ Cerci snorts. ØCould you please just not kill us before we get there?Æ ØAw, you never let me have any fun.Æ Regulus gives her a flat look that makes Cerci laugh. ØOkay, okay no death before the semester starts, you got it. You little nerd,Æ she reaches over to ruffle his hair. ØI donít think not wanting to die makes me a nerd,Æ he says, shoving her off. ØNo,Æ she bites her lower lip, smiling. ØBeing so excited for your first day of school does though.Æ ØIím not,Æ Regulus tries to ignore the blush he knows is heating up his cheeks. ØBesides, it isnít even really the first day. Itís move-in day. And Iím not excited about it.Æ ØUh-huh,Æ Cerci smirks, looking far too smug for Regulusís liking. ØOkay, mocking Regulus time is over now.Æ ØNooo!Æ she whines. ØBut thatís my favourite time!Æ He does his best to keep himself from laughing, the last thing he wants to do is encourage her. ØCould you just keep your eyes on the road? Please?Æ ØRelax itís redîoh wait,Æ she quickly slams her foot on the gas pedal, once again throwing Regulus forward. ØSeriously!?Æ ØSorry! I didnítîØ ØSee it, yeah, I know,Æ Regulus winces as he brushes his hand over his chest. Heís definitely gonna have a bruise. ØDidnít you play hockey? Shouldnít you be used to things slamming into you?Æ she adjusts the dial on the radio while she talks, turning up the music to inhuman levels. ØThereís padding in hockeyîkeep your hands on the wheel! Jesus.Æ ØWhat?Æ she shouts as she makes a sharp turn that has Regulus sliding into the door. He glares at her, turning down the music. Cerci sticks out her tongue. ØSpoil sport.Æ ØYou promised no dying.Æ Cerci lets out a huff but doesnít try to adjust the volume again. Out the window the street theyíre on is lined with old housesîbig ones, with wraparound porches and thick trunked trees. Some of the leaves have started to change colour, the first whispers of fall. It looks like a postcard. Regulus fidgets. Heís never lived anywhere this pretty. Heís afraid heíll stand out. ØHey, is your brother gonna be there today?Æ Cerci asks as the outline of the main campus starts to come into view and Regulus feels his pulse speed up. Okay, so maybe he is a little excited. And terrified. ØAm I finally gonna get to meet the infamous Sirius Black?Æ Regulus rolls his eyes. ØHeís not infamous.Æ Cerci gives him a sidelong look. ØHeís a little bit infamous.Æ ØWhatever. Yeah I thinkîhe said he was going to try to stop by soèbut I donít know. He might be busy with his own shit.Æ He knows that Cerci can hear the forced casualness of his tone, but she doesnít comment. For all that they tease one another, they both know where the line is. Know when it stops being fun. And Regulusís family is about as far from fun as you can get. The pathetic truth, is that Sirius is the only reason Regulus is here. Well, at least heís fifty percent of the reason. When Cerci decided on Godric U there wasnít really any other option for Regulus. But the reason he applied in the first place, the secret reason the school had always been his first choice (even though he told everyone it was because of its very reputable business school) was his brother. Sirius ran away when he was fourteen. No one ever phrases it that way, ever calls it what it was, but as far as Regulus is concerned that is exactly what happened. Sirius had been scouted by a prestigious athletics private school across the country. To this day Regulus doesnít know what he was expecting to happen when he told their parents, but it certainly wasnít for their mother to make clear that hockey was a hobby, not a career. Sirius was supposed to go on to study economics or business. Supposed to get internships at big tech companies before eventually coming to work for her. You only needed to talk to Sirius for five seconds to know that that was never going to happen. But then, their mother rarely didîtalk to them, that isîshe mostly just sort ofè talked at them. Anyway, Sirius informed her, in no uncertain terms, that there was no way in hell he was going to be following in her footsteps. He was NHL bound. End of story. Which their mother told him, in much the same manner, was a joke. Laughable. Childish. And there was no way she would ever pay for such a thing. But Sirius wasnít only good enough to be offered a place at the school, he was also good enough to be offered a scholarship. So one night, he packed his bags, and slipped out of the house. Going to one of his teammates who had also been recruited. Needless to say their mother was incensed. In fact, she was on the phone to the police to drag him back when their father finally stepped in. It was just a phase, he said, it would pass, and besides, it was still a school with a good reputation. Even if that reputation was for shooting pucks and not solving equations. So the phone was hung up. The police were not called. And Sirius was allowed to go off and follow his dreams. Regulus, on the other hand, was pulled out of Hockey, his life scheduled down to the minute with activities and hobbies his mother was certain would mold him into the son she wanted. The son Sirius should have been. She has, Regulus is certain, been endlessly disappointed by the results. ØOi! Space cadet? Earth to Regulus?Æ a hand waves in front of his face that he instantly smacks away. ØWhat?Æ he asks, looking over at Cerci. She smirks, gesturing out the windshield. ØWeíre here, you weirdo.Æ Itís only then that Regulus realizes theyíre not moving anymore. ØOh,Æ he says, looking up at the old buildings standing over them, at the hoards of kids with all manner of bags and boxes andèstuff. Most of them are surrounded by familyîhovering parents, little siblingsîand Regulus feels something in him ache. ØHey,Æ Cerci knocks their shoulders together. ØWhatís that look for?Æ He blinks, quickly wiping his face clean. ØJust thinking about all the things weíre gonna have to carry up the stairs.Æ ØUgh,Æ Cerci groans, head dropping back against her seat. ØI forgot about that. Canít we justè.magic it into your room?Æ Regulus snorts, undoing his seatbelt. ØYeah, you let me know how well that goes for you.Æ ØYou know,Æ Cerci says as she pops the trunk, following him out of the car. ØI really think you could be more supportive.Æ This area of campus is somewhat self-contained, with six old buildings all organized around a large courtyard. As Regulus slips one of his bags over his shoulder, he tries to figure out what each of them is. ØYou wrote down the residence info right?Æ he asks. ØErîyeah,Æ Cerci twists her arm around and it takes Regulus a second to realize what sheís doing. ØYou wrote it down on your arm?Æ he demands indignantly. ØWhat? This way Iím not going to lose it.Æ She shakes her arm at him. ØSee? Permanently attached.Æ Regulus rolls his eyes, though he canít help laughing a little bit. ØGod youíre nuts, you know that?Æ ØBut you love me anyway,Æ she leans over and gives him a big smacking kiss on the cheek. Regulus pulls a face that has Cerci snickering as she goes back to trying to decipher the hieroglyphics on her arm. ØI think youíre inèshitîno wait, thatís definitely an LîØ Regulus holds his hand up to shield his eyes from the blinding sun. ØSlyîtheîrin. Slytherin!Æ she looks up at him excitedly but Regulus only arches his brow. ØWhich isèwhere?Æ ØUhèÆ Cerci squints at her arm some more, spinning around. ØThere!Æ she points triumphantly to an old brick building with white pillars and an elegant cloth sign reading ØSlytherinÆ hanging above the door. ØGuess we could have just read the signs.Æ ØBut then we wouldnít have gotten the full experience of my wonderful map,Æ Cerci winks, grabbing a few more of Regulusís things and slamming the trunk shut. ØAlright sparky, lets go find your room.Æ ØI still canít believe youíre living off campus,Æ Regulus grumbles as they walk towards his dorm. ØOh cheer up, Iíll be here lots. And itís not as though we couldíve been roommates.Æ ØThey have co-ed floors,Æ Regulus argues, itís an old discussion, one theyíve been going over for the past few months now. ØYes, but not co-ed roommates. And if you wanted to live together so badly you could have gotten an apartment with me.Æ Regulus frowns but doesnít respond. Sirius had gushed about the residence experience, about how excited he was for Regulus to be hanging out in all his old haunts, how he was gonna teach him the tricks of the tradeîhow to scam the meal hall, how to sneak up to the roof, how to get change out of the washing machines. It isnít that Regulus really cares about any of that honestly, but itíd beenènice, for him and Sirius to finally have something to talk about again. So heíd signed-up for a room on campus and Cerci had abandoned him. ØNames,Æ thereís a boy standing in front of the entrance to the dorm, he has a clipboard in his hands that he doesnít look up from when he speaks, voice monotone. Bored. ØErîjust nameîone name, I mean, my nameîsheísîerîsheís notîØ ØRegulus Black,Æ Cerci cuts off his rambling. Regulus shoots her a grateful look while internally berating himself. He really isnít some meek blushing wallflower. Itís just that heís never gotten the hang of moments like thisî when he doesnít know the script. A pair of dark eyes flick up off the clipboard. They do not look friendly, which really isnít doing anything for his anxiety. ØBlack?Æ the boy demands, voice sharp. ØErîyes?Æ He feels himself being assessed and not in a particularly flattering way. Regulusís ears go pink. ØAs in Sirius Black?Æ As in Regulus. ØSirius is my brother.Æ The boy in front of him sneers. ØBrilliant, just what we needed at this school. Another entitled rich kid.Æ ØUhèÆ ØArenít you supposed to be the welcoming committee?Æ Cerci demands. ØCause I gotta tell you buddy, Iím not feeling very fucking welcomed.Æ The boy only scowls at her before turning back to his clipboard, eyes running down the page. ØThank God,Æ he says after a few seconds. ØYouíve been moved.Æ Regulus blinks. ØWhat?Æ ØYour dorm assignment has been moved. Youíre in Gryffindor now.Æ ØErîokay, and thatísîØ The boy doesnít even wait for him to finish before heís jabbing his finger across the courtyard. Regulus turns to see a building covered in clearly homemade paper banners, depicting lions with varying degrees of accuracy. ØOhèokay, uhîwhy was I moved?Æ he turns back to the dark haired boy who is still glaring at him. ØDo I look like fucking HR? Now get out of the way so I can help the people who actually live here.Æ ØOkay thatís it, you are a cuîØ ØYUP,Æ Regulus cuts Cerci off, sending her a please donít start a fight on our first day look. ØWeíre going. Thanks.Æ Mercifully, Cerci seems to have gotten the message, following Regulus as he starts making his way back across the quad. Though she does turn around when theyíre a few paces away to throw up her middle finger. ØReally?Æ ØHe deserves it. Dickhead.Æ As they get closer to Gryffindor it becomes apparent that someone is blasting music from a bluetooth speaker somewhere, Whitney Houstonís I Wanna Dance With Somebody growing louder the closer they get. Cerci leans into him. ØI already like this place better,Æ she whisper-shouts in his ear. ØGood, maybe you can still give-up your lease and move in with me.Æ She laughs. ØYouíve got jokes Black.Æ Thereís a short line this time before they get to another person with a clipboard. A blond girl, with freckles and a giant smile. ØHey guys, whatíre your names?Æ her eyes bounce between the pair of them, pausing on Regulus. ØSorry, have we already met?Æ She squints at him. Regulus does his best not to grimace, this is already feeling forebodingly familiar. ØNo.Æ She chews on her bottom lip. ØHuh, weird. Alright well, names?Æ ØRegulus Black,Æ he spits it out before he can start stuttering like an idiot again. The girlís eyes go wide as she drops her clipboard, snapping her fingers. ØYes! Thatís it! You look just like SiriusîDorcas! Hey babe, I found Siriusís twin!Æ ØPretty sure James is upstairs Mar,Æ a dark skinned girl with a shaved head and chunky purple glasses walks up to them. ØNo, like his actual twin.Æ The new girlîDorcasîstops in her tracks when she sees Regulus. ØOh shit.Æ ØRight?Æ Mar is practically bouncing up and down on the balls of her feet. ØUncanny no?Æ Dorcas steps closer, taking her glasses off and then putting them back on again, Regulus fidgeting under the scrutiny. He feels too hot, and like he doesnít know who to look at or what expression heís supposed to be wearing and his arms are also starting to get really tired from the stuff heís holding. Cerci presses lightly into his side. Sheís probably about to say somethingîrescue him again. This is why he needs her here. He has absolutely no fucking clue what to do with all these people. ØAre you sure that isnít actually Sirius?Æ Dorcas asks finally. ØItís his brother,Æ Cerci interrupts, drawing the girlsí eyes towards her. ØBrother?Æ Dorcas says at the same time that Mar goes: ØOh, the last name makes a lot of sense now.Æ Dorcas pulls back to look at her. ØHis last name is Black and you didnít make that connection?Æ Mar rolls her eyes. ØI got distracted.Æ ØBy?Æ ØHis face! Heís wearing Siriusís face!Æ Figures. That even Regulusís face would belong to Sirius. Now itís Dorcasís turn to roll her eyes, turning to Regulus. ØSorry about herîØ ØHey!Æ ØBut you do look a crazy amount like him. Even for brothers.Æ ØSirius is taller.Æ That voice is new. All of them turn towards a third girl, leaning against the wall. Sheís wearing all black, from her boots to the scarf covering her head, and She's eyeing Regulus in a way that makes him squirm. Like she can see right through him. ØHuh,Æ Mar turns back to him. ØYeah, you might be right.Æ And isnít that just a metaphor for his whole life. A shorter Sirius Black. Never quite measuring up. Literally. Hilarious. ØCan I get my room number and like, key or whatever?Æ he adjusts the bags in his arms. Marís eyes go wide. ØOh! Yeah! Wow, sorry, Iím all over the place.Æ She turns to the folding table behind her, grabbing an envelope and a tote bag full of stuff. ØYour roomís on the third floor, number 302, keycards in here,Æ she shakes the envelope. ØAnd this,Æ she holds up the tote with an excited grin. ØIs your Frosh package.Æ ØErîthanksîmaybe just, put it on top,Æ he shakes the stuff in his arms. ØRight, you got it.Æ ØGreat,Æ he grunts. ØOkay, well, Iím just gonnaègoèÆ he starts walking towards the front doorsîor at least he hopes he does, he canít really see over the stuff in his arms. ØSee you around baby Black!Æ Dorcas calls after him. Regulus does his best not to cringe. Regulus and Cerci donít talk on their way up to his room, or even when he struggles to get out his keycard, dropping half his stuff on the floor in the process. Picking it up is honestly a waste of time because as soon as Regulus is inside he just drops it all on the floor again, walking over to one of the bare beds and collapsing onto it. ØThis was a mistake,Æ he mumbles into what is almost definitely not a clean mattress. He feels Cerci sit on the bed next to him. ØOh come on, it wasnít that bad.Æ ØYouíre a dirty liar Greengrass.Æ She laughs softly. ØAnd youíre a drama queen. It was fine, itís always awkward meeting people.Æ ØEspecially when your brother is apparently a fucking celebrity.Æ She pats his leg comfortingly. ØItís just cause itís new. People will get over it.Æ Regulus groans, flipping over so heís on his back, staring up at the cracked ceiling. ØI forgot what this was like,Æ he says finally. ØWhat what was like?Æ ØExisting in the same space as him.Æ Fucking exhausting. Thatís what itís like. Regulus groans again. ØThis was a mistake.Æ Cerci rolls her eyes, getting off the bed and offering him her hand. ØItís going to be fine, you want to see him, remember? You miss him. Now get up so we can get the rest of your crap.Æ Reluctantly, Regulus takes her hand. Thatís the thing about Sirius. He does miss him. Heís been missing him for years. But that doesnít make it any less difficult to stand in his shadow. It doesnít take them long to bring the rest of Regulusís things up. It's not as though he has much. A few clothes. Laundry basket. Andî ØHoly shit, what did you pack a whole library?Æ Cerci huffs, as she drops a box full of books onto the floor by his bed. ØI tried. Dad wouldnít let me take his first editions, though.Æ Cerci gives him a look, her face flush and sweaty from the last few runs up and down the stairs. ØThat is both the most obnoxious and nerdiest thing I have ever heard you say.Æ Regulus laughs, the pair both collapsing back onto his bed. ØMoving is terrible.Æ ØAgreed.Æ ØLets never do it again.Æ Cerci snorts. ØWhatíre you just gonna live in this dorm room for the rest of your life?Æ ØSure, why not? Itís got all the basics.Æ ØThe basics?Æ ØA roof, a floor, wallsîØ ØWow the barís really high for you isnít it?Æ ØWhat can I say, Iím a simple man.Ø Cerci makes a disbelieving noise, looking as though sheís going to say more when there is the unmistakable sound of a keycard unlocking the door. By the time Regulus has lifted himself up onto his elbows a tall, buff, blond boy is pushing his way into the room. The boy drops his bags inside the door, blowing out a breath as he runs a hand over his face, lifting his baseball cap up and then placing it back down before he catches Regulusís eye. His face instantly breaks into a friendly smile. ØSup roomie.Æ Regulus isnít quite able to manage a reply. Itís not that he hadnít known, somewhere in the back of his mind, that he was going to have a roommate. Itís just that he had tried very, very hard to forget that fact. To postpone the inevitable panic that he was now feeling at this moment. ØIím Evan,Æ the boy leans over all the stuff now covering their floor and extends a very large, tanned, hand to Regulus. ØErîØ he awkwardly pulls himself the rest of the way up. ØRegulus.Æ The blond boy arches his brow. ØRegulus? Never heard that one before. Very cool,Æ his eyes drift over to Cerci. ØHey, Evan,Æ again he extends his hand. ØCerci,Æ she smiles when she shakes it, clearly amused. ØDamn, two for two eh? Where are yíall from? LikeèNew York?Æ he picks his bags up and throws them down at the foot of the other bed. ØWhy New York?Æ Cerci asks. Evan shrugs, tossing them a smile over his shoulder as he starts to unpack. ØJust seems like the sort of place where people might be called Cerci and Regulus.Æ ØIím gonna take that as a compliment.Æ ØYou should,Æ he winks at her. When Evan smiles you can see freckles dancing across his cheeks and nose, eyes bright blue. He looks like some sort of cartoon version of a boy. Like he belongs on a nineties sitcom or an advert for hair gel. Very wholesome. Very all-American. It makes Regulus instantly wary. Heís met boys like this before. ØSo, are you two a thing then?Æ Evanís question snaps Regulus out of his thoughts, Cerci snorting beside him. ØNo,Æ she says bluntly. ØNot even a little bit.Æ ØAlright, jeez,Æ Regulus grumbles. ØTry not to sound too excited about it.Æ ØAw did I hurt your feelings?Æ she makes a little pouty face and Regulus shoves his hand into it, pushing her down onto the bed. Cerci giggles, eventually fighting him off. When Regulus looks up again he finds Evan leaning back against the desk on his side of the room, slight smile on his face. ØWell thatís good news bro, we can be each otherís wingmen huh?Æ he holds out his fist which Regulus stares at for a solid five seconds too long before realizing heís supposed to bump it. He does. Itís incredibly uncomfortable. ØWeíll work out a system yeah?Æ Evan says as he returns to his unpacking. ØA system?Æ ØYíknow,Æ he waves his hand vaguely in the air. ØA way for me to let you know when Iíve got someone over and vice versa, so we donít get in one anotherís way.Æ He walks over to his closet, unceremoniously dumping a pile of clothes inside before shutting the door. Regulus isnít sure why Evan bothered taking his clothes out of his bag if thatís all he was planning to do with them, but he figures that is probably one of those comments better kept to himself. ØIf we have someone over?Æ he looks at Cerci who is definitely trying to communicate something to him with her eyes but he isnít getting it. ØSorry did youîshould I not have brought someone over before asking you? She was helping me move soîÆ ØWhat? No,Æ Evan laughs. ØI meant like over over, yíknow?Æ Regulus does not. ØYou just repeated the same word twice.Æ Evan gives him a bemused look before his eyes slide over to Cerci and Regulus feels hot shame course through him. He knows that expression. The one people make when heís missed something. Done something odd. Or awkward. ØHeís talking about sex,Æ Cerci says bluntly. Itís one of the many things Regulus has always appreciated about her. ØOh,Æ Regulus says flatly, ears still burning. ØUnderstood. Fine, yeah, system would be great.Æ Not that Regulus sleeps with people very often. Or ever. The idea of being that close to someone, that intimate, trusting them that muchîmakes his skin crawl. Plus, it doesnít help that heís still functionally in the closet. I mean, technically he came out, a suggestion from his therapistîshould have been his first clue that it was a bad ideaîbut he pretty promptly slid back in. You think you canít take something like that back, but with enough self-denial anything is possible. ØWell,Æ Cerci elbows him, Øshould we start unpacking? Then we can head over to mine and îØ But at that moment the door is flung open for the second time, and Regulus swears if he somehow has a third roommate he is grabbing all his stuff and going back home. But then he sees a familiar head of long dark hair, and a pair of twinkling grey eyes. ØSirius,Æ he breathes, his brother grinning at him as he walks into the room. A second later Regulus is off the bed and launching himself into his brotherís arms, the pair of them teetering a bit with the force of the hug. Sirius grips him just as fiercely. ØHey Reg,Æ he says softly. Sirius calls. He video chats. But the truth is, he hasnít really been home since he left the first time. Before Cerci, Sirius was the only person who ever really understood Regulus, who never looked at him like Evan just had. The only person Regulus felt genuinely at ease around. ØHoly shit,Æ Evan says as the pair of them finally pull apart, Sirius keeping his arm over Regulusís shoulders. ØYouíre Sirius Black.Æ Sirius turns to him, megawatt smile plastered on his face that looks somehow both genuine and mocking at the same time. ØOnly on my good days.Æ Evan has a few inches on Sirius and yet somehow heís still managing to give off the impression that heís looking up at him. But then, Sirius has always had that effect on people. ØIím EvanîEvan Rosier, Iím gonna be playing with youîwell, hopefully.Æ ØNo shit,Æ they do a sort of half-handshake half-handslap thing that Regulus doesnít entirely understand. ØWhat position?Æ ØIím a D-man,Æ Regulus is pretty sure Evan puffs his chest out a little. Siriusís eyes running over him appreciatively, no doubt taking in his height and size. ØNice, we could use more defence. Peteís a good goalie but he canít do it all on his own. Speaking of hockey,Æ Sirius gets a dangerous look in his eyes. ØYou guys meet your RA yet?Æ Regulus has no idea what that has to do with hockey. ØEr no? Should we have?Æ But Sirius only squeezes his shoulder before pulling away. ØPerfect. He hates making a scene, calling attention to himself, asking favours. All the things Sirius canít help but do. ØI donít believe weíve met?Æ She smirks at him. ØNot officially, no. Iím Cerci.Æ Siriusís face brightens. You wound me.Æ Regulus gives him a flat look but Sirius only smiles as he lets the door close behind Cerci, starting off down the hall at a brisk pace. ØKeep up children!Æ he sings over his shoulder. Evan and Sirius seem to naturally fall into step with one another, already talking about potential strategies for the upcoming season and what Evan should expect going in. Regulus attempts to tune them out. He misses playing. He was never, perhaps, quite as fanatical about it as Sirius butèheíd been good at it. And it had beenèeasier, to connect with people, on the ice. When they all had a shared goal. When he knew who was on his team and who wasnít. He sometimes thinks life would be much simpler if everyone was wearing jerseys. At least then youíd know who you could trust. ØHey,Æ Cerci says quietly as they walk past the common room that divides the floor in two. ØYou alright?Æ Regulus makes sure to smile at her when he says; ØYeah, totally, justèyíknow, itís a lot.Æ Eyes going to the back of Siriusís head. ØAlright comrades,Æ Sirius stops in front of one of the dorm rooms. ØMay I introduce to you, captain of the Godric Gryffins, RA for Gryffindor Tower, and all round swell guyîØ he looks at Evan who seems to understand what is expected of him immediately, slapping his thighs to create a drumroll. ØJaaammeeesss-mother-fucking-Pottterrrrr.Æ Sirius kicks the door hard, causing it to fly open, revealing a room very similar to Regulusís except that it only has one bed instead of two, and standing in the middle of it, looking something between shocked and annoyed, is a boy with brown skin and wild hair who is, distressingly, shirtless. Regulusís eyes instantly drop to the floor, cheeks heating up. ØFor fuckís sake Sirius! I told you to stop doing that, I swearîØ he freezes, noticing the other three people for the first time. ØUhèhave you brought a tour group to my room?Æ and then. ØWaitîare they letting you lead tours now? Whatíre they trying to traumatize the frosh?Æ Sirius snorts, waltzing into the room and unceremoniously plopping himself down on the bed like he owns the place. ØI brought you some of your most important constituents,Æ Sirius gestures to them as they tentatively file in behind him. James rolls his eyes. ØIím an RA Sirius, not a politician.Æ Sirius puts his hands under his chin and bats his lashes. ØWell if you were a politician youíd have my vote.Æ ØFlattery will get you nowhere Black.Æ ØI have twenty years of experience that say otherwise.Æ Regulus is still staring at the floor. Itís not that heís never seen James Potter before, he has. In the background of video calls, or in pictures with Sirius. And yes, sure, he had had some fleeting thought then that James Potter was attractive, though it was largely overpowered by the feeling that James Potter was replacing him. The brother that Sirius wanted. That he always should have had. But seeing James Potter in person is another experience entirely. And seeing James Potter shirtlessè ØJames, may I introduce you to the gangîthat oneís Evan Rosier, heís gonna be our new D-man.Æ Regulus thinks Evan might actually be blushing, rubbing the back of his neck in an Øaw shucksÆ motion. He wonders if Evanís Øsweet farm boyÆ routine is genuine or if under it all heís actually the asshole he looks like. James steps forward, giving Evan the same approving look Sirius had. ØRosier, eh? What did they call you back home?Æ Evan sputters for a moment as though surprised to have been asked a question. ØRosy.Æ Sirius makes an excited noise from the bed. ØRosy!Æ ØDo you like it?Æ James asks, looking at him thoughtfully. ØWe could make Roz work too.Æ ØReally James?Æ Sirius wrinkles his nose. ØYou would take Roz over Rosy?Æ ØIím just saying, we have options.Æ ØDisagree.Æ Evan gives them a smileîhis teeth irritatingly straight and white. ØI meant it as a compliment.Æ ØFor some reason I find that hard to believe.Æ Cerci leans into Regulusís side and whispers: ØSorry, Iím so lost, what is happening?Æ ØItís a hockey thing,Æ Regulus whispers back. ØHockey players are big on nicknames.Æ Cerci makes an ØohÆ shape with her mouth and then: ØIs the dog thing also about hockey?Æ Regulus bites the inside of his cheek to keep his smile from getting too big. ØNo, pretty sure thatís all them,Æ he nods at Sirius and James. ØWould youîstop distracting meîI havenít finished giving my wonderful introductions,Æ Sirius says, shoving James off as he tries to pet him. James laughs, making something in Regulusís chest wiggle. He presses down on his sternum like he can make it stop. ØSorry,Æ James coughs, trying to return some element of composure to his voice. ØGo on then.Æ ØThank you. ØBut youíre probably gonna be seeing a lot of me because of this one,Æ she elbows Regulus who isnít sure where to look. Making eye contact with James seems too intense but looking away too rude, so his gaze ends up hovering somewhere around Jamesís shoulder. ØRegulus Black,Æ James smiles at him. ØIn the flesh.Æ And before Regulus can stop himself he hears himself say; ØNot as much flesh as you. I feel overdressed.Æ Jamesís eyes widen and then dart down to his torso, as though heís only just realizing that he isnít wearing a shirt. How nice for him. ØSorryîI uhîØ James starts rummaging through his room, looking, presumably, for something to wear. ØI got sweaty helping some people move-in and I was in the middle of changing when someone kicked my door open.Æ He gives Sirius a pointed look. ØWow, who would do that? What an asshole,Æ Sirius gasps. James pulls a shirt out of a pile of laundry in the cornerîitís red with the gold outline of the Godric mascot on the front. Regulus doesnít realize how intently heís watching until Jamesís eyes flick up and stumble right into his. Once, when heíd been on the ice, Regulus had gotten slammed into the boards, the impact so hard that for a moment his whole body had felt paralyzed. He couldnít breathe, he couldnít think, his chest a black holeîsucking everything in and letting nothing go. Inhale. Inhale. Inhale. Like he would never get enough air again. That's what it feels like. Being looked at by James Potter. A punch to the gut. ØHey! Earth to Regulus?Æ Sirius snaps his fingers in Regulusís direction and suddenly the world speeds up again. Sorry?Æ Regulus struggles to get his concentration back, looking down at his brother whoís still sprawled across the bed. Sirius gives him a weird look but doesnít ask any questions. ØBut my parents are still in town and if I skip out on family dinner my mum will never forgive me.Æ ØAw, Rosy are you a big mamaís boy like Prongs here?Æ Sirius asks, kicking at Jamesís legs. James swipes at himîtrying to cuff the back of his headîbut Sirius rolls out of the way, sitting up on the other side of the mattress looking far too pleased with himself. Regulus expects Evan to be embarrassed but he isnít. ØOh yeah, big time. My momís likeè the greatest.Æ Something twists in Regulusís stomach. The weight of a hand gripping his shoulder. Donít let me down. He looks over at Sirius but his brother doesnít react. Expression neutral. ØWell, if youíre done early you can stop by.Æ He turns back to Reg now, brow raised in question. Regulus fidgets. Heís tired and the last thing he wants to do is spend the night with a bunch of people who donít know him and will only feel obligated to talk to him because heís Siriusís little brother. ØHeíll be there,Æ Cerci answers before he gets a chance. ØI can introduce you to the rest of the boys and like, the whole crewîitíll be fun.Æ Regulus does his best to manage a smile. ØAwesome.Æ ØYou know,Æ Regulus hisses when they walk out of the dorm room fifteen minutes later. ØI donít remember giving you permission to speak for me.Æ Cerci rolls her eyes. ØYou were going to say no.Æ ØOf course I was going to say no!Æ ØWhat? Why would you say no?Æ Both Cerci and Regulus turn to Evan, having somehow forgotten he was still there. ØOoh, give me a sec okay?Æ She moves a few paces down the hall. ØHello?Æ she answers, turning away from them. ØYeah, he is.Æ ØHeís been drafted by the Red Wings, right?Æ Regulus shakes his head, fidgeting. ØDunno.Æ They donít talk about hockey much. ØMan thatís sick. Do you play?Æ An unwelcome ache. ØUsed to.Æ He isnít looking at Evan. Evan who is the opposite of him in every wayîbig and strong and easy going. Regulus doesnít trust him. Doesnít trust anyone that happiness comes easily to. ØPotter seemed cool too.Æ Regulus really wishes Evan would stop trying to talk to him. Really wishes Cerci would get off her phone and assure him that she isnít going to abandon him tonight. Really wishes his anxiety would give it a rest. Thereís no need for him to feel like scratching off his own skin. Nothing is wrong. Donít let me down. ØWhy do you think they call him Prongs?Æ Evan is still rambling on. ØNo idea.Æ Regulusís teams always called him ØJuniorÆ. ØYouíre a hockey player.Æ Regulus really had felt more himself on the ice. On a team. He knew the script in a locker room. What to say, how to act. Sometimes that had meant cutting off parts of himself and hiding them away. And heíd done it willingly. Honestly, heís never stopped. Something hurt flickers across Evanís face. Regulus can feel Cerci looking between them. ØUh, sorry, did I miss something?Æ ØNo,Æ Regulus says, Evan making a vague noise of agreement. ØFuck.Æ Turns out, horror of all horrors, Cerci has already found herself a job. First training shift tonight. Regulus offered to pay her to go to the bar with him instead but she seemed to think that was not a long term solution. ØYou wanna be my sugar daddy?Æ ØWill that get you to go to the bar with me?Æ Sheíd laughed at that. Not that he let himself think the word crush or anything even remotely close to it. They were twelve, shared a locker room. A team. Sometimes he couldnít stop himself from looking at him. Just his face. Or his hands. His eyes. Sometimes he thought Christopher was looking back. It was the worst kind of torture. He had wanted him painfully and earnestly and absolutely incompetently. Of course neither of them ever did anything. Ever said anything. Though sometimes they would stand a little closer to each other than they needed to. Hug a little longer after a goal. Once or twice Christopher sat next to him on the bus, when they were going to away games. Their legs pressed to together. Even just that had felt illicit. Had felt dangerous. Regulus still remembers the first time he heard Christopher call someone a faggot. Still remembers the way it twisted him up inside. According to facebook Christopher has a girlfriend now. He isnít sure why all this time later heís suddenly thinking about him again. He doesnít think itís a good sign. ØRegulus? Regulus wake up.Æ He groans, burying his face further in his arms. ØDude, címon. I gotta go and if youíre not conscious before I leave this room youíre gonna be late.Æ Regulus groans again, trying to shrug off the hand gripping his shoulder. ØYouíre killing me here.Æ There's a pause and then Regulus experiences what feels like an earthquake. ØAhh!Æ he jolts awake, grabbing hold of his desk for stability. ØFinally,Æ Evan lets him go, moving to his side of the room and picking-up his duffle bag. Regulus blinks. He doesnít remember falling asleep. ØFive-thirty.Æ ØShit, I forgot to set an alarm.Æ ØYeah, I figured,Æ Evan is standing by the door, looking at him funny. ØYou need to go to sleep bro.Æ ØI was sleeping,Æ Regulus says testily, starting to pack up his notes. Heíll bring them with him to work and look at them on his lunch break. Evan sighs. He canít very well walk into the office in yesterdayís clothes. ØPractice. First game is coming up.Æ Another grunt from Regulus. He needs to do laundry, something else he hasnít had time for. Acquaintances. It just seems unnatural that some guy heís waved at awkwardly in the hallway might see his underwear. Regulus shakes his head. These are not important thoughts right now. Weird or not weird he needs clean fucking clothes. ØOkay, well, see you later I guess,Æ Evan says, sounding already half-way out the door. ØYeah, see you.Æ Regulus isnít late for work, but itís a near thing. Sweaty and out of breath as he slides into his tiny cubicle. He sighs, pointlessly trying to push the curls out of his face. There is no place on earth more depressing than a cubicle. Of that he is certain. If purgatory exists it looks like these three, flimsy, grey walls. A stack of papers slam down on Regulusís desk making him jump. The deliverer hovering over him with a scowling face and shoulder length black hair. One of the many joyous discoveries Regulus made on his first day was that the upperclassman who had been such a dick to him on move-in day is also an intern here. ØYouíre late,Æ Severus says. ØIím not.Æ Because he isnít. ØYouíre not early which means youíre late.Æ That logic is fundamentally flawed, but Regulus doesnít bother pointing that out. Heís not able to stop himself from rolling his eyes though. This kind of nitpicking is a daily occurrence. Largely due to the fact that Severus feels very strongly that Regulus doesnít deserve to be here, and in fairness, heís not wrong. These internships are meant for senior undergraduate students. Regulus is the youngest person here and everyone knows itís because of who his mother is. ØWowîplease tell me youíre an English major?Æ Regulus doesnít know if James is being sincere or not. ØNo,Æ he says finally. The truth seems like the safest answer, even if the question is sarcastic. ØBusiness.Æ Jamesís eyebrows shoot up. ØBusiness? What? Why?Æ Regulus has to bite his tongue to keep from saying because my mom told me to. It sounds just a tad too pathetic. ØNot many jobs in literature.Æ Itís clear from the way he wrinkles his nose that thatís not good enough for James. ØNot many jobs in the NHL either, you still got to try though, if itís something you feel passionate about.Æ Regulus swallows, turning back to his notes. ØNot all of us have the privilege of trying.Æ ØThatís not true,Æ James says dismissively, which makes Regulusís hand curl a little tighter around his highlighter. ØYes, it is,Æ gritting his teeth. ØTrying implies the possibility of failure, not everyone is allowed to fail.Æ James goes quiet, and Regulus underlines something on the page in front of him just to make it look like heís actually reading, ignoring the way he can feel Jamesís eyes on him. ØWho told you you werenít allowed to fail?Æ James asks finally. Donít let me down. Nails digging into his shoulder. Donít let me down. Thereís always a silent word at the end of that threat. An empty space they both know how to fill. Donít let met down. Again. Regulus slams his notebook shut. ØIím tired,Æ he says shortly, not looking at James as he starts to shove things into his bag. ØI think Iím going to go back to the dorms.Æ ØWoahîhey, waitîØ But Regulus is already on his feet, jerkily throwing his bag over his shoulder as he does everything but sprint out the door. The sun has set by now and itís cold without the light, Regulus wrapping his arms around his chest. Campus is lit by older street lampsîshort iron stems with round orbs at the top. It makes him feel like heís in some sort of artsy short film. ØRegulus! Hey, Reg wait up!Æ Regulus grits his teeth, very pointedly not waiting. ØHey, hey,Æ James jogs backwards in front of him, cruelly forcing Regulus to once again put-up with his face. ØListen, Iím sorry.Æ ØFor what?Æ Regulus asks flatly, keeping his gaze forward and not slowing down a step. ØErèÆ James falters. ØYou shouldnít make apologies you donít mean. Sets a bad precedent.Æ ØIím notîØ but he cuts himself off, clearly struggling to keep up with Regulusís pace. ØI am sorry. I justèneed you to tell me what I did?Æ Regulus only shakes his head. ØYou didnít do anything.Æ James makes a scoffing noise. ØOh come on, clearly I did something.Æ ØNope.Æ ØRegulus,Æ he steps directly in front of Regulus now, stopping them both firmly in their tracks. Itís a bit aggravating if Regulus is being honest. Itís not something heís used to. At least not directed at himself. People generally handle Regulus roughly. Heís a bit annoying, or so heís gathered. Never quite able to pick up on what everyone else isnít saying. Always second guessing himself. Quiet. Sullen. The opposite in every way to Sirius. Itís a few seconds before Regulus is able to force himself to speak. ØItís not you,Æ he says grudgingly, really wishing they werenít having this conversation, that James had just let him study in peace. He hasnít got the energy for any more people today. ØIím justèI really am tired.Æ James seems to consider him for a moment, those dark eyes running him over in an unhelpfully intimate way. ØNo. If we were eight I would make you pinky promise.Æ He wiggles his pinky finger for good measure. ØJesus Christ,Æ Regulus steps around him and starts walking again, a few seconds later James falls into step beside him. ØFollowing me?Æ James snorts. ØI mean, for starters, you never promised.Ø Regulus sighs. ØAnd,Æ James goes on, Øwe kind of live in the same building.Æ Regulus instantly feels his cheeks heat. He grips the straps of his bag but heís pretty sure that makes him look like a little kid so he quickly drops his arms, but now they just feel awkward, hanging uselessly at his sides. Having a body is truly a nightmare. ØSoooo,Æ James says in a bit of an exaggerated voice, Øhowíre you liking it so far?Æ Regulus looks at him. ØIt?Æ ØYíknow,Æ James waves his hand around. ØCollege.Æ ØUmèfine?Æ James nods. ØGood. Thatís good. And your classes areèinteresting?Æ Regulus makes a face, and before he can stop himself; ØIíd honestly rather we go back to not talking thanèdo whatever this is.Æ James stops, laughing so loud Regulus is almost certain theyíre going to get a noise complaint. ØBut I promise I care about this one.Æ Regulus can tell that James is trying not to smile and he wonders if heís being mocked. He gives James a skeptical look. ØOkay?Æ ØHowís Rosy doing?Æ Regulus blinks, he hadnít been expecting that. ØEvan?Æ ØYeah.Æ Heís so thrown by the question that heís not entirely sure how to answer it. ØHeís fine? Why wouldnít he be fine?Æ James shrugs, looking out in front of them. ØHeís from a pretty small town, I think him and his family are really close, so being so far away, in a city, itís a big change. Be satisfied with not embarrassing himself too badly or offending James so deeply that he wants nothing to do with himîwhich has happened a few more times than Regulus would like to admit to. He should just put his head down and push forward. And yet, for some reason, he hears himself say: ØHowís Lily?Æ If James is surprised by the question he doesnít show it, but then, Regulus supposes people probably ask him about Lily all the time so heís used to it. He doesnít even know him. And besides. Heís notègay. Well, not outside his head anyway. Itís better that way. ØYou know,Æ James says suddenly, startling Regulus out of his thoughts. ØItís good you know. Weíre good. Happy, I think? ØYeah, she thought I was a bit of a prick,Æ and then, after a second; ØTo be fair, I was a bit of a prick. Especiallyîshe had this friend, this guy, and the pair of us never got along. If in another life James would be one of the boys standing beside him. ØWe got over it though,Æ James goes on. ØMe and Lily I mean. ØCan a relationship be too easy?Æ ØI donít know,Æ he chews his bottom lip. ØBut sometimes itís like weíre just really good friends and not actuallyèÆ he shakes his head and then laughs. Really appreciate it xx Chapter 3 Chapter Summary In the words of Jack Kerouac: ØDon't use the phone. People are never ready to answer it." Chapter Notes tw homophobic language Things you maybe wanna know: Cochlear Implants: A cochlear implant is a small electronic device that electrically stimulates the cochlear nerve (nerve for hearing). The implant has external and internal parts. Enforcer: is the dude whose job (unofficially) is to fight the other dudes more than every other dude fights the other dudes (this definition was brought to you by Evan Rosier) The Frozen Four: is the playoffs of college hockey (or if you wanna be technical about it: the NCAA Men's Ice Hockey Championship) See the end of the chapter for more notes Part I Remus He doesnít know whatís woken him up. His eyes heavy as they blink open. Itís late, still dark out. He turns over, reaching blindly for his phone, it takes a few seconds before he finally manages it, a too bright screen aggressively informing him that itís 3:06 AM. He sighs, dropping the phone beside him and scrubbing at his face. He doesnít know whatís woken him up. But he has a feeling it was Sirius. Maybe briefly flicking the hall light on and off, maybe his headlights as he pulled into the driveway outside Remusís window. Not that he needs to do anything. Sometimes Remus swears theyíre connected, a thin thread tied around his pinky and leading all the way to Sirius. When Sirius pulls Remus feels it. And Sirius pulls an awful lot. Heís one of the most restless people Remus has ever met, always buzzing with some barely contained energy. Itís part of what makes him such a good athlete. Remus never understood the appeal of sports before watching Sirius play. Years of his father desperately trying to make him care. Forcing him onto teams that, quite frankly, had no idea what to do with a deaf kid. It all seemed silly and pointless to him until Sirius. It's like watching a piece of theatre when he's out on the ice. Every movement of his body, expression of his face, is so articulate. So passionate. Making you root for him. Making you believe, for a moment, that a stupid hockey game matters. He swings his legs over the side of the bed, yawning before he gets to his feet, taking his blanket with him as he stumbles out of his room and into the hall. The door to Siriusís room is open, a clear indication that he isnít there, so Remus keeps moving. Fairly certain he knows where to find him. Sure enough, the window of their living room is cracked open, the dark silhouette of a boy just visible, sitting on the roof outside. It doesnít seem to matter how many times their landlord replaces the screen or how many times the neighbours complain, Sirius inevitably finds himself on the roof. Remus supposes brooding on the sofa isnít quite as romantic. With his comforter still wrapped around him Remus waddles forward, opens the window all the way and fumbles through. Sirius turns to him, says somethingîhis mouth moves at least îbut Remus is too busy trying not to fall to his untimely death to make out what it is. He huffs when he finally gets outside, leaning back against the brick wall behind him. He finds Sirius staring at him, a smile on his face. Your blanket is going to get dirty, he signs. We have a washing machine. Not that itís cold enough to really warrant a blanket but Remus just isnít in the mood to face the world without it yet. Sirius shakes his head, smile tucked into the corner of his mouth as he brings a cigarette to his lips, getting ready to light it. Remus frowns. Youíre not supposed to be smoking. Sirius rolls his eyes, and in an exaggerated, and somewhat pouty gesture, returns the cigarette to its packet. Happy? Remus gives him a flat look. Ecstatic. That makes Sirius laugh, Remus can tell because his shoulders shake a little, lips twitching, slightly parted. He's not sure what laughter sounds like, but he knows that on Sirius it's something he wants to touch. Wants to reach out and run his fingers along. Something Remus wants to hold in the palm of his hand, and keep with him in case he ever needs to look at something beautiful. Whatíre you doing out here? Remus asks eventually. Couldnít sleep. Itís Remusís turn to roll his eyes. Worked that out, thanks. Why canít you sleep? Siriusís head wobbles from side to side, looking out at the night. They live close to campus, on a street filled with old houses that were probably nice at some point but have been put through the ringer by decades of college students and are now scuffed and peeling. When they first moved in together Remus used to search Siriusís face for signs of disappointment. Remus is all well and good, but heís no James Potter, and as long as heís known James and Sirius theyíve always been roommates, attached at the hip. But no matter how hard he looked he never did find any disappointment, maybe because they always knew James was going to be an RA, like his dad. Just like they knew he would be captain of the Griffins like his dad. And marry his high school sweetheart like his dad. Remus knows a thing or two about complicated father-son relationships, and he worries about James. Thereís a point when everyone realizes that their parents arenít heroes, theyíre just humans. But for most people that point is somewhere around the age of sixteen. He worries that one day that reality is going to come crashing down on James, and itís going to devastate him. Remus leans over and gives Sirius a shove. Just enough to get his attention. Sirius turns, flipping him off. Why canít you sleep? Remus repeats, with as much emphasis as his hands can muster. Sirius stares at him for a long timeîtoo long reallyîRemus has always found his gaze a little hard to hold. Eventually, however, Sirius seems to give in. Iím worried Iím fucking things up with Regulus. Actually, he signs Regulue, fingerspelling has never been Siriusís strong suit, but now feels like emphatically the wrong time to point out grammatical errors. Why? Remus asks. Heî but then he stops, dropping his hands and making a frustrated face. He starts again: I donít know how to be his brother. There is thisèwallèbetween us. Stuff we donít say. Sirius bites down on his lower lip, hesitating for a moment before adding; Things happened. It got bad for a bit. And I should have been there, you know? I should have been there but I wasnít and I just donít see how heís ever going to forgive me for that. Remus is ashamed to say that he desperately wants to know what Sirius means by Øthings happenedÆ. But he does manage to stop himself from being completely insensitive. Has Regulus said anything to you? About being angry or upset? No. Butè Thatís part of the stuff you donít say? Remus guesses, earning him a nod. Exactly. Remus watches Sirius for a moment. His eyes are a strange shade of blue, pale and soft and almost grey. Remus has never seen anything like them before. Or at least he hadnít, until Regulus showed up. Have you considered that maybe you should say it? Theèstuff? he asks finally. That makes Sirius smile, though itís not quite sincere. Itís not that easy. It is too that easy. Youíre just making it complicated. But Sirius shakes his head. Reg heèhe doesnít talk about things. Doesnít talk about anything really. Remus arches his brow. Unlike you, Sirius Black, the open book. That earns him another middle-finger. Remus waits for a second, incase Sirius wants to actually say something, and when he doesnít he shoves him again. Talk to your brother, stupid. Sirius rolls his eyes, biting back a smile. Yes mum. For a moment they both sit still, hands in their laps as they look up at the sky. Remus yawns, it must be nearing 4:00 and if he doesnít go back to his bed soon heís going to end up falling asleep on this roof. He turns to Sirius, to tell him so, when he notices that Siriusís mouth is moving. He blinks, confused. Surely Sirius isnít speaking to him? He knows better than that. But then Remusís eyes trail down to the phone resting by Siriusís hand, screen lit up with the name of the song currently playing. Heís listening to music. Remus wonders if itís been playing this whole time? Often, people view the world in absolutes. Things are or they arenít. Itís yes or itís no. Right or wrong. In most instances those beliefs are incorrect. The world much messier. Deafness, is no exception. Itís not as simple as hearing or not hearing, but more like youíre on a sliding scale. Everyone falling somewhere slightly different. Itís not that Remus canít hear music at all. He can, if it has a strong bass, a heavy beat, if the volume is turned all the way up and the speakers are good. He loves music, honestly, sometimes heíll lie on the floor of his room next to his speaker and just let it flow through him. The thing is, he doesnít think he could describe music in sounds. He wouldnít even know where to start. But the way it feelsèmusic is all feeling for Remus. He nudges Siriusís shoulder, to get his attention. Are you a good singer? Sirius smirks. No. Awful. Remus frowns, not quite believing him. He might not be able to hear Sirius, but he can see him. And heís captivating when he singsîface bright, eyes wide, cheeks a little flushed. Remus doesnít understand how any part of that could be awful. Here, Sirius signs. Iíll show you. What? Remus uses his voice because Sirius is already halfway through the window. If he responds itís not in anyway that Remus can understand. For a few minutes he just sits there, alone on the roof, confused about how Sirius thinks heís going to show him how he sings? But then Sirius is back, climbing through the window with an irritating amount of grace. He sits crossed-legged in front of Remus who mirrors his position, though it takes significantly more shuffling for him due to the blanket. Sirius is grinning as he holds up a pair of chunky black headphones. How are those gonna help me hear you sing? Remus signs, skeptically. Trust me, already reaching over and shoving them on Remusís head. Remus bats away his hands, huffing as he adjusts the headphones himself, the comforter falling from his shoulders and pooling around his waist. The cool fall breeze tickles his bare arms, making him shiver, and when he looks back at Sirius he has a soft expression on his face. What? Remus demands. Nothing, __ just l__k _____ Remus squints. Sirius is speaking now instead of signing and Remusís brain is too sleepy, and the roof is too dark, for him to lipread worth a damn. I just look? he repeats back. I just look what? Sorry, I didnít catch the last word? Sirius gets a funny expression on his face before shaking his head, spots of colour appearing on the tops of his cheeks. Nothing. Of course that wordís clear. Siriusî But Sirius reaches out and covers Remus's mouth, much to Remus's dismay. He tries to pull away, and when that doesnít work, he licks Sirius. Sirius only laughs, dropping his hand just to wipe his palm on Remusís shirt, causing him to make a disgusted face. Itís your ____. Remus fixes him with an unimpressed look. Spit is spit. Itís gross no matter whose mouth it comes out of. Sirius shakes his head, still smiling as he looks down at his phone and then back up at Remus. Ready? Probably not. But he sees Siriusís thumb move and thenîthe volume is clearly turned all the way up, Remus can feel the pulse inside his ears, the earphones vibrating. Maybe itís sound, itís hard for him to distinguish sometimes. The vibrations and the noises, they meld together. But thereís a beat, and Remus feels himself start to bob along. He doesnít realize heís closed his eyes until Sirius taps him on the shoulder, prompting him to open them. Heís got that soft look on his face again. Ready? Sirius repeats, this time signing. For what? Sirius smiles. For my singing. He still doesnít understand, but then he sees Sirius start to sign again; Good times for a change See, the luck I've had Can make a good man Turn bad There are moments of stillness between phrases and it takes Remus too long to realize that Sirius is signing in time to the rhythm humming through his headphones. Heís signing the lyrics. So please, please, please Let me, let me, let me Let me get what I want This time Heís singing to him. Haven't had a dream in a long time See, the life I've had Can make a good man bad So for once in my life Let me get what I want Lord knows, it would be the first time Lord knows, it would be the first time Sirius lowers his hands slowly, the humming in Remusís head disappearing, the pair just looking at one another for a moment. See? Sirius signs finally, small smile on his face. Awful. Remus only shakes his head, feeling ridiculous and emotional and unable to stop himself from clumsily giving Sirius a hug. I think youíre a beautiful singer, he murmurs into his neck. Itís a long time before he eventually pulls away, Siriusís arms reluctant to let him go. Heís just glad he isnít crying because that would be embarrassing. Send me the song will you? he says, finally sliding the headphones off his head and handing them back to Sirius. The other boy nods. Yeah, of course. Before he can stop himself Remus lets out a huge yawn, making Sirius laugh againîat least he thinks so. Come on, lets get ___ to ___. Iím not that tired, he tries to argue, not ready to leave this space. This moment. Unfortunately, his protests are undermined by the fact that he yawns again almost immediately after. Sirius shakes his head, crawling over and herding Remus back through the window. Remus complains a few more times but theyíre feeble attempts. The truth is, heís absolutely exhausted. Sirius follows him all the way to his bedroom, shoving him down on the mattress after quickly brushing off some of the rooftop debris from his blanket. Good night Sirius signs once heís in bed. And Remus has the ridiculous desire to pull him back, to ask him to stay. Sirius? Sirius turns to him, silhouette lit by the light spilling in from the corridor. For a second theyíre just frozen like that, staring at one another, before Remus remembers why he called him back in the first place. Thank you This time Siriusís smile is almost shy. Anytime. He leaves not long after that, and Remus falls asleep dreaming about Sirius singing. Remus and libraries have a complicated relationship. On the one hand, books are incredible. You can go anywhere and be anyone and do anything. Which was especially thrilling to a young Remus whose nervous parents let him do very little. Not that he blames them necessarily, but books became his escape. They still are. And the idea that buildings filled with books existîfree books at thatîhas always felt like a little bit of magic to Remus. On the other hand, volume control is not his strong suit. He likes bars, clubs, concertsî places where everything is loud so he doesnít have to worry that heís shouting. He gets anxious in every day life. Self-conscious. Cringing when he orders at a coffee shop, avoiding speaking in class unless he absolute has to. The library is the worst though, a place where youíre not just supposed to speak at a normal volume, but at a quiet one. He does have hearing-aids and they help he supposes. But mostly they just make everything very noisy. Nothing ever feels clearer it just feels louder. He hates them honestly, rarely puts them in when he's not at home. It was his father who was adamant he get them and adamant he wear them and adamant that he did not need an interpreter even when he entered the regular school system. Middle school was very hard for Remus. He expects high school would have been as well, if he hadnít met his boys. As he walks towards the main campus libraryîreferred to by most people as the Ro, short for RowenaîRemus feels that familiar sense of unease. As long as no one talks to him itíll be fine. As long as he doesnít have any questions or need a librarian to help him find a book or want to buy a coffee, itíll be fine. He exhales, consciously unclenching his fists. Heís only a few steps from the doors when a group of boys off to the side catch his attention. Heís not sure why at first, until he sees a familiar head of dark hair and quite nearly dies of shock. Sirius is an excellent student, truly, Remus is positive heís never failed a test or gotten anything below an A on an assignment. But the library is not a place where one finds Sirius Black. In fact, Remus doesnít think heís ever actually seen Sirius sit down with a book and study in the entire time heís known him. After a few seconds of staring Remus realizes that it isnít Sirius. Itís Regulus. Thereís a slightly buff looking guy getting in his face, while three other boys linger back, watching. It could be nothing, but Remus starts slowly moving closer anyway. And as he does, the figures start to look more familiar. The big one, the one in Regulusís face, he knows him. Heís on the hockey team. Nicolai Mulciber. He starts walking with a little more purpose now. Nicolai is in the year above him and according to James and Sirius is incredibly bitter that James was named captain instead of him. Heís also just generally an absolute prick. Remus tries to make out what heís saying to Regulus but he can only see his profile and apparently heís shit at enunciating because Remus canít pick out a single word. Now, the problem is, itís possible that Regulus is actually having a good time, just hanging out with friends. Heís frowning, but in fairness, Remus isnít sure heís ever seen him not frowning, or at the very least not looking incredibly concerned about something. But Remus canít help but see Nicolaiís looming stance as menacing. So, despite having absolutely no idea what heís getting in the middle of, Remus decides to open his mouth: Regulus? Regulus startles, eyes flicking to Remus just as Nick turns around, leer on his face, looking every bit the entitled jerk he is. Remus has, thankfully, only had to interact with him a handful of times. Most of which were less conversations than Remus simply being trapped in the same room as him at a party. Are you okay? he asks Regulus. Maybe he should have gone with something a bit more subtle? Oh well, too late now. Regulus looks like heís going to be sick. Still, when he moves his mouth Remus is pretty sure the words that come out are: Fine. Iím fine. Which is quite clearly a lie. Remusís mind races, trying to come up with some excuse to get Regulus out of this situation as easily as possible. They need Sirius honestly, heís the one with the daring escape plans. Remus is about to speak again when he sees the younger boyís eyes cut-across to Nicolai. He canít hear you, Regulus says, andthen: Talking louder isnít going to fix that. Remus snorts as he turns his attention to Nick, who appears to be running his mouth. Remus squints trying to make the words out. The problem is, he has a very difficult time understanding people he finds dull. Itís as though his brain simply refuses to expend the energy on them. ____. W___ do ___ ___ Remus? Remus just stares at him blankly, which seems to amuse Nicolai. Hello? he waves his hand obnoxiously in Remusís face. Anyone home? Do you even know what Iím saying? heís grinning, near identical smiles blooming across the faces of his friends. Clearly everything Nick says is peak comedy. Remus considers responding until he remembers that he has absolutely no desire to talk to Nicolai Mulciber, so he turns his attention back to Regulus. Have you eaten yet? he finds himself asking. Regulusís face scrunches in confusion. What? Remus hates this particular question. Because sometimes people mean ØwhatÆ as in Øwhat did you mean?Æ and sometimes they mean ØwhatÆ as in, Øwhat did you say, I didnít hear you?Æ but since Remus didnít hear himself either, heís never really sure which one it is. Have you eaten yet? he says more forcefully. Force is the only way he can really understand volume. More force louder, less force quieter, but itís not an exact science. Judging by the way Regulus winces and Nicolai smiles, the ØwhatÆ in question, did not require greater volume. Lovely. No, Regulus says finally. Or at least, Remus is pretty sure thatís what he says, heís muttering a little bit. Me either, trying for a casualness he doesnít feel. How åbout we go grab something yeah? he nods over his shoulder in the direction of the meal hall, hoping his eyes are communicating something along the lines of ØIím trying to rescue you just go with it.Æ Regulus opens his mouth as if to speak but then quickly shuts it again, eyes going wide as he looks at Nick, which Remus takes as a sign that Nicolai is, unfortunately, speaking again. Reluctantly, Remus looks at him. ___ cute, a date. Oh come on Regulus, donít look so surprised. Didnít your brother tell you heís living with a fucking faggot? Remus doesnít blink. Heís been out since he was fifteen. Out and deaf at an all boys private school with a large population of hockey players. ØFucking faggotÆ is practically a term of endearment compared to what heís been called. Donít be jealous Nick. Iíd ask you to lunch but I prefer men who can read. He returns to Regulus and finds a pair of terrified eyes staring back at him. He actually thinks the other boy might have taken a step back, not that theyíd been standing particularly close to begin with. Which is when it hits Remus that Regulus isnít scared of Nicolai. Heís scared of him. Oh. He hadnít seen that coming. Not when Sirius had accepted his sexuality without batting an eye. Not when he had photos of Sirius and James at Pride with goofy grins and rainbows painted on their faces. Heíd mentioned to them once, that he goes with his mum every year, which is maybe a bit embarrassing but sheíd taken him when he was timid and uncertain at fifteen. And then again when he was sixteen and seventeen. And somehow it had become a tradition. Then one summer James, Sirius and Peter drove down to go with them. And that had started a whole new tradition. So you see, of all the people he expected to look at him like that, Regulus Black was not one of them. Iím ___ _ungry, Regulus is speaking quickly now and partially to the floor making it hard for Remus to keep upîthough he expects the slightly nauseas feeling in his stomach isnít helping either. I have __ ____ soè Nick is smirking at him, triumph radiating off him, though Remus isnít exactly sure what heís won. It takes Remus a second or two to slot words into place, to fill in the blanks he couldnít read. But he understands the sentiment. Regulus does not want Remusís help. In fact, Regulus does not seem to want anything to do with Remus period. He nods his head jerkily. Okay, cool no problem. See you around yeah? And then he starts walking, feeling a little numb, not quite sure what just happened. It takes ten minutes before he realizes heís walked right past the library and started going home. He doesnít turn back. Thereís an uncomfortable itch burrowing in Remusís chest and he presses down on his sternum trying to get it to stop. If Regulus has a problem with Remus Sirius wonítîhe wouldnít cut Remus out right? They live together for godís sake! But Sirius loves his brother, has been desperate to have him back in his life almost the entire time Remus has known him. And if Regulus makes Sirius chooseè Theyíre blood he sees the words in his mind. All you are is some loser pathetic enough to have a crush on his best friend. He shakes his head. This is stupid. Heís being ridiculous. Regulus isnít going to go around making ultimatums. Remus will just stay out of his way and everything will be fine. Normal. The same as always. Unfortunately. The itch disagrees. A few hours later Remus sits at the desk in his bedroom, staring at his laptop screen and feeling fidgety and nervous the way he always does before calling his parents. It would be okay if it was just his mum, but as soon as his dad enters the picture thingsèchange. Remus makes his father uncomfortable, thereís no way around it. His mother tries to deny it but Remus doesnít know why, anyone with eyes can see it. The thing is, his father feels guilty. A notification pops up on the screen, asking him to join the meeting. Remus lets out a deep breath before he accepts. A few seconds later his parents appear on the screen, the iPad held at a horrendously low angle that is doing neither of them any favours but makes Remus smile. His mother is a small woman, maybe five feet tall on a good day, she has frizzy strawberry-blond hair that usually has no fewer than three pens in it, and skin thatís covered in freckles îshe loves to garden so sheís almost always in the sun. Her smile is toothy and a little too big for her face and one of Remusís favourite sights. Hey baby, howíre you doing! Remus can see his father stiffen as his mother signs. This is why he hates these calls. Iím good mum, yíknow, just getting back into the swing of things. Swing. His father repeats, overly exaggerating the word. What? Youíre losing your Sís. Do you need me to find you a speech pathologist out there? It might help to have a few sessions, itís been a while since youíve seen anyone. When Remus was younger it was very important to his father that he learn, not just how to speak, but how to speak ØproperlyÆ. Remus spent years in speech therapy trying to learn how to make his mouth form all the sounds he couldnít hear. Sís have always been a particular weakness of his. Something about themîtheyíre too soft, too slippery, he canít ever seem to grasp them. NoîIím juSt tired, thatíS all it iS, he makes an extra effort to try to pronounce his ØsísÆ though heís not sure he manages it. Long day? his mother signs. He gives her a weak smile. His mother has always been his cheerleader. Whether that was dedicating herself to learning sign language the moment they found out Remus was deaf or hanging rainbow flags up on every available wall after heíd come out. Her enthusiasm for, well, everything, is contrasted by his fatherís distain for everything. Remus canít figure out how they ended up together. Or why they stay together. Though he does feel a bit guilty about thinking the latter. Very long, he signs back, doing his best not to think about Regulus. What about you? Are you getting ready for the winter season? Preparing the plants? Her face lights up, there is nothing Hope Lupin likes discussing as much as her plants. For a solid five minutes Remus gets to have the very delicate task of protecting tulip bulbs explained to him, there are many enthusiastic hand movements. No one signs quite as brightly as his mum. So, his father cuts in eventually, pulling his shoulders back. And since youíre under twenty-five youíre still covered. His father is looking at him like this ought to mean something. Which, sure, health insurance seems like a good thing. Probably. Congratulations. His father seems a bit miffed by this response. That word stings and Remus isnít sure he can exactly explain why. But I donít wantî Use your voice. Remus bites his lower lip, resisting the urge to snap back. His mother must say something he misses because his father turns to look at her. Most people canít understand sign language, he canít rely on it. Heís heard his father say that countless times. Remus hadnít meant to sign, he knows better than that. It had just slipped his mind for a moment. He was tooîangry isnít the right word. Nervous maybe? Frustrated? He doesnít know how to explain what heís feeling. He wasnít prepared for this at all. Weíve never talked about implants before, he manages finally. It ___ never an option before. The insurance wouldnít cover the whole thing and we couldnít pay the difference. No _____ discussing it. Ièappreciate what youíre offering, but I donít want implants. His father looks genuinely shocked by this, itís maybe the most emotion Remus has ever seen him show. Donít be ridiculous. This would give you the opportunity to live a normal life. This isnít anything new. His dad says, or implies, things like this all the time. But it still makes him flinch. I have a great life, Remus pushes back. I like being the way that I am, he almost says, but doesnít. Because he knows itís a loss cause with his father. And because he doesnít want to have him list all the reasons he thinks Remus is wrong. Youíre being childish Remus. And shortsighted. Iî But his mother interrupts, holding her hands up in a clear demand for a ceasefire. Enough, she signs. And when Lyall opens his mouth she repeats aloud: Enough. Turning back to Remus: I know this is a lot, but maybe you should think about it, okay? Instead of just shutting down the idea right away? Itís worth considering baby, donít you think? No. He doesnít want fucking surgery. Doesnít want all the therapy heíll have to go through afterwards. Heís never expressed any interest in implants. For his dad to justîjust make this decision for himîthis life altering decisionîis so fucking out of line. But the word appears annoyingly in his mind. what if it works? what if you can actually hear Sirius sing? Remus, his motherís hands drag his attention back to the present. Remus slumps back in his chair, feeling suddenly exhausted. Fine, he signs eventually, causing his mother to give him a small smile. The call doesnít last long after that. Part II Regulus Regulus wakes up to a call from his mother. ØAre you still in bed?Æ are the first words out of her mouth after he answers the phone. His mother has this incredible trick, where she doesnít raise her voice, but somehow manages to make whatever it is that youíre doing sound so pathetic and stupid that you instantly feel as though youíve been flayed. Naked and exposed and burning with shame. ØI have the morning off,Æ he whispers weakly, shooting a look over at Evan who is still asleep in bed. ØDo you need something?Æ heís still whispering, afraid the surrounding rooms might hear. ØI have to need something to call now?Æ ØNon maman, thatís not what I meant.Æ But she ignores him.
