Regulus is barely able to nod his head in response, to let Evan know heís heard. Itís justîitís been a long time since anyoneís wanted him on their team. ØThank you,Æ he manages finally. Part III Peter Peter is fucking tired, gym bag swung over his shoulder as he heads out of the arena. Technically heís not supposed to be here, but James and Sirius made copies of the key when they were Frosh and somehow convinced the maintenance staff not to rat them out. So they all use the gym after hours from time to time, and the rink. Itís a bit hard to run drills by yourself when youíre a goalie, but he can still go through the basics. He likes being here when itís emptyîwith no pressure, no one to compete against. Itís why he doesnít usually tell Sirius or James when heís coming. Itís hardèstanding next to them all the time. It always has been. Remus seems to be able to handle it better than Peter, never jealous or insecure. Peter isnít sure how he manages it. He tries, but heís never quite able to rid himself of that feeling of inferiority he gets when he stands in their light. Both Sirius and James are going to the NHL, but Peter, with any luck, will end-up working at some advertising firm. A glorified used car salesman. This, college hockey, this is it for him. So he wants to make it good. And he wants to win. He might not be as crazy as James or Sirius, but that doesnít mean he wants to be a loser. He yawns as he pushes out into the chilly fall night, starting towards his carîthe only one in the lotîwhen he spots someone coming towards him. Someone familiar. ØJames?Æ he stutters to a stop, James startling, eyes finding Peter for the first time. ØPete?Æ ØWhatíre you doing here?Æ James shrugs, shifting his weight back and forth between his feet, clearly agitated. ØJust wanted toèburn some energy.Æ He doesnít have his gym bag with him, heís wearing jeans and a t-shirt with his backpack hanging off his shoulder. If Peter had to guess, heíd say this was a last minute decision. Very last minute. ØAre you okay? Did something happen with Lily?Æ Jamesís eyes narrow. ØWhat? No. Why would you ask that?Æ ØUh,Æ Peter blinks a few times, feeling like heís missed something. ØBecause you seem a bit upset so I thought maybe youíd had a fight with your girlfriend orèÆ he pauses for a second and then; ØDid something happen with Sirius?Æ James throws his arms up in exasperation, though thereís a slightly desperate edge to it. ØNo. Nothing has happened with anyone. Lily is great and Sirius is great and Iím great. Weíre all really great okay?Æ ØOkaaaayyyy,Æ Peter looks around like heís going to spot someone who can rescue him, but the parking lot is, of course, still empty. ØIím like especially great actually,Æ James goes on. ØIím fan-fucking-tastic! I mean, why wouldnít I be? I have you and Sirius and Remus and a super hot girlfriend andîand our team is awesome this year.Æ Peter nods in agreement, because their team IS awesome this year. ØWeíre definitely going to win the Frozen Four and weíre gonna beat the shit out of Salazar and itís going to be epic. So literally nothing could be wrong,Æ he exhales, and Peter wonders if heís taken a single breath this whole time. ØUhîI meanîno. Obviously I meant no.Æ ØYou said yes.Æ ØYeah wellèitís opposite day. Surprise!Æ James does not look amused. ØOkay, listen,Æ god Peter really needs to learn to think before he speaks. ØYou donít cross like, serious boundaries, youíre justîyouíre Jamesîyou take charge and you like to make sure everyone is okay and sometimes that means you kindaèyíknowèsolve problems no one asked you to.Æ James just looks at him for a moment before shaking his head, letting out a humourless laugh. ØFuck.Æ ØJames, really, what happened?Æ he adjusts the strap of his bag which has started digging into his shoulder. James just shakes his head again. ØI donít know whatís happened, IîI donít even know why I went to the library in the first place. I donít know why I wantedèI donít know what I wanted,Æ he tangles his hands in his hair. Peter honestly has no idea whatís going on. ØThe library? What does this have to do with the library?Æ But James ignores him. ØI just feelèreally off lately,Æ he kicks at the gravel by his feet. Peter waits for him to continue, to explain properly, but when he doesnít Peter shuffles a little closer and knocks their shoulders together. ØYou bring your car?Æ James shakes his head. ØWalked.Æ Peter arches his brow. ØFrom the Ro?Æ ØYeah,Æ he shrugs. ØLike I said, needed to burn off some energy.Æ Peter frowns, eyes running his friend over before he jostles him again. ØLets go get some food yeah?Æ ØBut I was going to workout,Æ James says lamely, itís a weak argument and they both know it. ØSure,Æ Peter says. ØBut Franís has twenty-four hour breakfast, and I feel like chocolate chip pancakes are gonna make you feel better than working out.Æ James gives him a small smile. ØYeah alright.Æ ØCome on,Æ Peter nods over his shoulder towards his car before he starts walking. ØIíll drive.Æ James snorts. ØI still canít believe you have a license.Æ ØI canít believe Sirius has a license,Æ that makes James laugh out right, and Peter feels a little proud. ØHonestly, he must have bribed someone.Æ James shoots him a sly look out of the corner of his eye. ØWith money or sexual favours?Æ ØKnowing Sirius? Probably both.Æ James laughs again, itís a juvenile noise, reminds Peter of being back in high school. If you could summarize his adolescence in one sound it would be James Potterís laugh. ØYou know,Æ Peter starts, not quite able to meet Jamesís eye. ØFor what itís worth, I usually do need you to fix my problems.Æ James snorts, slinging his arm around Peterís shoulders and pulling him into his side. ØThanks Pete.Æ James is a little bit taller and leaner than Peter, something undeniably possessive about his touch. James Potter has arms that were built to hold people. Peter isnít sure if he always understands how addictive that is. Or how desperate they all are to feel it. Chapter End Notes Hello lovelies! In case you don't know and wanted to, the song Sirius is listening to at the start of the chapter is Please, Please, Please Let Me Get What I Want by the Smiths :) I know these are very "set-up" chapters but I promise things are gonna start getting juicy, especially in chapter 5. He's like a Beckham or Michael Jordan type See the end of the chapter for more notes Part I Peter Two Years Earlier ( First Year) Itís a big deal being on the Griffins. Playing in the NCAA on one of the best teams. Itís something Peterís father boasts about to his friends who are always surprised. Peter? they say, like theyíre sure theyíve heard wrong, little Peter? He plays hockey? He got a scholarship? He used to find their surprise insulting, like he hadnít spent every waking moment of his childhood on the ice fighting for this. This opportunity. This chance. Sure, itís not the NHL, but itís close. Itís so fucking close. And heís getting it with his two best friends. Just for a few more years, theyíre still together. Still on the same team. The house they walk up to is bigîthree floors, maybe fourîthereís a white plastic table on the lawn already piled high with red solo cups. A giant flag with the Griffinís logo strung across one of the first floor windows as a makeshift curtain. Peterís stomach squirms with nerves. Sirius grabs him, pulling him close and whispering right into his ear ØRookie party, rookie party, rookie party!Æ Peter laughsîokay, it might be more of a giggle but heíll never admit it îhe playfully pushes Sirius away even though he doesnít mind the closeness. Likes it actually, when Sirius and James touch him, like heís theirs. Because he is. They can already hear the music inside, spilling out like the house just canít hold anymore, the three of them pushing and shoving one another as they walk up to the front door. It swings open almost as soon as they knock, their captain standing in front of them in a button up shirt and jean shorts, grinning. He has a joint behind one ear and a bottle of Jack Danielís in his hand. ØBoys,Æ he says, gesturing them in like Bacchus in all his glory. ØWelcome.Æ The party starts out normal enough. Thereís music and drinking. Lots of drinking. A keg in the living room that makes Peter feel like heís in some kind of teen movie. Sirius, of course, does a keg stand, balancing on his hands while James feeds him the funnel. People applaud when he gracefully drops back down onto his feet and Sirius, never one to miss an opportunity to play to the audience, takes a bow. Then, catching Peterís eye, walks over and gives him a big kiss on the cheek. So maybe it isnít a normal party. Maybe itís an incredible party. Peterís new teammates are all laughing around him, James and Sirius putting on a show but always looking for him in the crowd, giving him a smile or a wink, like theyíre letting him know itís all for him. The three of them their own team. They met in Mini Mites, Peter only five years old, fumbling around on the ice barely able to hold his stick. Even then heíd been in awe of James and Sirius. They were the only ones who seemed to have any control. And when Peter fell over they were the ones to pick him up. Somehow it snowballed from there. From hockey practices to playdates and birthday parties. They moved up together, always side by side. Spending hours on the frozen pond behind Jamesís house practicing and fucking around. Sometimes Peter misses those days. Back when there was less pressure, back when the three of them were equals. At least as much as they ever could be. It feels different now that James and Sirius have been drafted and Peter hasnít. Like maybe he isnít allowed to love the sport as much anymore. Like itís sad that heís trying so hard at something heís already failed at. Like even though theyíre standing next to him, in a way theyíve already left him behind. Itís maybe two hours into the night when the older boys herd them all into the living room, the rookies on one side and everyone else on the other. ØBeing team means something,Æ Mac, their captain, starts saying. ØItís about having each otherís backs out on the ice and here in this room. Itís about being able to trust that the guy next to you is gonna do what it takes. Is gonna sacrifice fucking everything for his boys. Itís about being able to trust that your teammate isnít some weak little bitch who breaks the minute something gets hard.Æ Peter is already tipsy at this point, so how the cup gets in Macís hand he doesnít know. But suddenly itís there, being held up like a chalice. ØYou think itís coach who decides whoís on this team? It isnít. Itís fucking us. Weíre the ones out there with you on the ice. Weíre the ones you have to fucking prove yourselves to.Æ Anything, Peter thinks. Iíll do anything. Mac grins at them, the boys behind him grinning too, nudging one another with their elbows, looking almost excited. Like theyíre waiting for something. Then Mac brings the cup up to his mouth and spits into it. The red solo cup makes its way through the whole team before coming back to the captain, who eyes the rookies with a hunger thatís suddenly making Peter nervous. ØSo lets see huh? Whoís gonna get the bitch cup?Æ Now, it should have occurred to him earlier, if not immediately, that someone was going to have to drink that. But it hadnít. Not until that moment. Mac prowls up and down the line of rookies. ØEenie, meenie,Æ he says slowly. ØMiny,Æ his eyes flick over James and Sirius, not pausing for a second. ØMoe.Æ It takes Peter similarly too long to realize that when Mac stops in front of him, he isnít going to move again. ØHe looks like a little bitch,Æ Mac holds the cup out to Peter. ØLets see if he really is one, huh?Æ Peter is what is often referred to by coaches and scouts and other hockey parents who feel the need to give unsolicited commentary, as undersized. Too small to be a hockey player. Thereís always another implication there as well though, one perhaps summed up in Macís eloquent speech. That if heís too small to be a hockey player, maybe heís also too small to be a man. Peterís hands shake when he takes the cup. ØYouíre not actually gonna make him drink that are you?Æ James is trying to sound jokey, light, but Peter can hear the strain in his voice. Mac turns his shark like smile on him. ØWhat happened? ØI mean, you probably grew up rooting for them right? Since Salazar is in your hometown.Æ Evan struggles for a minute, to get his brain back on hockey. ØErîyeah, I suppose. A bit.Æ Actually, itís very weird. The first day heíd shown up on campus and seen all the red and gold and lions, it had felt unnatural. Like a betrayal. Taking a painfully long time to undress, to shower, pausing between each step. ØI was wondering if youíd wanna come over to my place and hang out?Æ Crouchís hands freeze and he frowns at the wall in front of him. ØYou want me to come over to your house?Æ Evan shrugs. ØMy momís ordering pizza and we can just like, chill and play Xbox or whatever?Æ Crouch still looks like heís struggling with the concept. ØItís late,Æ he finally manages. ØYou can stay over, the whole basement is basically my domain, the pull-out couch is actually really comfortable, just ask Connor,Æ and when Crouch keeps frowning. ØOr I can drive you home. Whichever.Æ Thereís a long moment of silence then. Isnít really sure what to do with Barty Crouch at all, honestly. Which is maybe why he keeps finding himself looking at him. Standing near him. Putting himself in his way. Desperate to understand. Evan has never felt this feeling before. Never felt this horrible sick feeling in the pit of his stomach, like something very bad is happening and he canít do anything about it. Canít do anything but watch. Has never felt this feeling before. Of wanting desperately to reach out and touch someone and knowing that he canít. Heíd thought Crouch was an asshole. ØDid you know butterflies are covered in scales?Æ Evan snorts, theyíre in the field behind his house, throwing a football back and forth. ØFuck off.Æ "They are! ØI mean, the scales are made of chitin which is a transparent proteinîØ ØMini dragons,Æ Evan repeats. Barty rolls his eyes. ØYeah okay.Æ Heís sitting in his stall, popping his mouthguard in and out of his mouth. ØNow, I know what youíre thinking,Æ Moody says, standing at the front of the room. Itís just a Tuesday. The two teams skate around their respective sides of the rink, warming up, no one crossing the centre line. The arena is packed, every seat in the place taken. Knees bouncing up and down as his eyes dart about. ØYou okay?Æ Evan asked him on the bench. Heíd gotten a jerky head nod in response and nothing else. Now, on the ice, he can still feel it. The effortless way Barty usually makes it up the rink is stilted. He isnít connecting on passes, is missing scoring opportunities. Itís like his head is somewhere else. Evan wants to grab him and pull him off the ice, wants to force Barty to tell him whatís wrong? Whatís happened? But all he can do is watch. Like a car crash. Evan doesnít know if the fact that Barty is hurting is as obvious to everyone else as it is to him but he thinks it must be. Heís favouring his left side, shying away from hits, and moving far slower than he normally does. But despite all that, heís still a phenomenal player, still outsmarting almost everyone on the ice. It only takes him a few seconds to score a goal. He sees it happening and he doesnít. Itís fast. Everything in hockey is fast. Split seconds. One minute the other teamís forward is pulling his stick back for a slap shot right in front of the net. Evan racing, trying to get to him. Embarrassed that heís given him this opportunity. And the next Barty is standing in front of the net. Heís standing in front of the net. Evan doesnít understand how he got there. He shouldnít be this far back. He shouldnít be blocking a fucking slap shot. The Griffins have scored another goal. The game is once again tied. The ref blows his whistle but Evan doesnít even hear it. Sliding down to his knees next to Barty. Bee?Æ ØAhhhhh, fuck, fuck oh fuck.Æ ØBee look at me, youíre okay, itís okay, youíre gonna be okay.Æ ØNo. No. No.Æ ØI promiseîØ ØNo. Donít make me break another one. Enough. Fuck his wallet. Part II Remus Remus hates pamphlets. Itís a very specific hatred heís aware. Still. The cover of the one currently in his hands shows a smiling middle-aged couple with some vague nature scene in the background. At the bottom of the page it reads: Learn about cochlear implants and if they may be right for you. Who I am. Okay. He grits his teeth, forcing himself to be more honest than he would like to be. If all goes well, youíll be discharged after a few days of observation. And then the therapy, Remus signs. Dr. Pomfrey nods. And then the therapy. Because it wonít be like natural hearing. Heíll still have to train himself to use it. Make sense of it. And he waits. But the door doesnít open. Part III James James is sitting on the floor, leaning against his bed, Lily lying on top of it. ØGood.Æ Thereís a moment of silence, the pair still clinging to one another before Lily finally speaks: ØThis is really the end, huh?Æ James wills himself to hold it together this time. ØYeah,Æ he manages. ØYeah, I think maybe it should be.Æ And then heís alone. He sits on his bed and he looks out at his room and he tries to figure out what to do. Because this thing has happened. This big thing. And yet somehow everything looks the same. He still has readings he has to do. Still has class tomorrow. Practice after that. But a few hours ago he belonged to someone. And nowè he throws his glasses onto the bed beside him, scrubbing at his face. And now heís alone. He knows, of course, that you donít need to be in a relationship. But the thing is, he likes it. Heís always liked it. Always had girlfriends, even in elementary school. Likes the intimacy of it, the connection. Likes having someone to take care of. Being Lilyís boyfriend has made him feel so good for so long. And he knows that it maybe isnít healthy to want to be with another person the way he does. Knows that heíll get over it eventually. And maybe find someone else. But at the moment all he feels is the loss. Is the empty space beside him in his bed. Is the silence where Lilyís laugh should be. He should probably call Sirius. He puts his glasses back on, pulling his backpack off the floor and rummaging through the side pockets when he feels something heís not suspecting. A wallet. ØOh shit.Æ He pulls out the small brown leather wallet that he was supposed to give to Regulus. The one with his fucking room key in it. ØOh shit.Æ He briefly closes his eyes. Rosy should have been back ages ago so Regulus must have gotten into his room by now but it certainly isnít helping with the whole Ømake Regulus stop yelling at meÆ objective. He looks down at the wallet again, passing it back and forth between his hands before, for some reason, opening it. In the first card slot, the one with clear plastic over it, is Regulusís student card. With his picture and everything. He isnít smiling, of course. But there is just the tiniest hint of nervous joy in the corner of his mouth, curls neatly arranged around his face. James finds himself smiling down at it. Thumb swiping over the card. Itís a sweet picture. Without thinking about it James closes the wallet and shoves it in his back pocket before looking around for his sneakers. Heíll go give it to Regulus and heíll apologize for today and maybe, hopefully, get them back on good terms. Because he wants them to be friends. He wantsî Well, he wants them to be friends. Itís not a far walk down to the other end of the hall. He passes by the common room, someoneís watching a movie, a few other people sitting on the disgusting couches and chairs, laughing and talking. James remembers the time in first year when Sirius hid a cheese sandwich in the couch cushions. Called it a social experiment, wanted to see how long it would take anyone to notice. James is pretty sure itís still there. When he opens the door to the other side of the hallway he finds himself stuttering to a stop. Down at the end, in front of their door, he sees Reg and Rosy, talking. Rosy has his gym bag thrown over his shoulder and it looks, horrifically, like he may have just gotten home. Meaning that Regulus has been stuck outside his room this whole time. ØFuck,Æ James hisses under his breath, taking another step forward, ready to call out to them whenî Regulus moves suddenly. He goes up on his toes, grabbing Rosy by the back of the neck and pulling him down into a kiss. Like a real kiss. Not for a laugh. Not fucking around. A proper kiss. For a moment Jamesís entire brain short circuits. Thoughts blank as he watches. As he watches and he accepts, properly, for the first time. That he doesnít want to be Regulusís friend. That seems to be enough to wake him up. He turns around quickly, barreling back through the door, whole body feeling kind of numb, unable to process a single feeling or thought as he basically runs away. He doesnít realize, until heís standing in his own room again having a proper panic, that he still has Regulusís wallet clutched in his hand. Chapter End Notes Listen, y'all said you wanted jealous Jegulus ... But things are happening. Feelings are springing Miscommunication is in the air! And he still has his wallet!!! I'm excited :) Thank you, as always, for reading! ØI just didnít know what to do, thatís all. He looks back at Peter. ØDid you drug him?Æ ØWait what?Æ Remus demands, Sirius sitting forward too, eyes intent on James. But James doesnít look anywhere but at Peter. Quiet. Not trusting himself to do anything but sit there. He flexes his hands on the table to keep them from curling into fists. ØWhat did you think it would be like?Æ he asks coldly. Because what do you say after that? Itís Peter who breaks the silence eventually. ØI donít know what you want me to do James. Iím sorry. I did the wrong thing. I know I did. One mistake and weíre justîØ voice cutting itself off, eyes blinking away the dampness. ØAnd youíre just never going to speak to me again?Æ Even hearing Peter say it, it sounds ridiculous. ØI could get kicked off the team,Æ he whispers. And James understands, he really does, why that matters so much. Understands how hard Peter has fought to be here. How much of his life has lead to this. James gets it. But he canít forget what it was like, staring at Evan on that rooftop, the 911 operator in his ear, feeling certain this kid was never going to wake up. ØPlease donít make me go back there.Æ James shakes his head, letting go of Siriusís hands so that he can wrap his arms around him. ØI wonít,Æ James swears against the other boyís temple. ØI promise I wonít.Æ And he doesnít. ØHey dad,Æ James holds the phone up to his ear as he makes his way across campus. ØWhatís up?Æ The trees are all orange and red, the colours at their brightest, looking almost burnt around the edges. It's pretty, especially when the sun shines through them. There are groups of people spread out on the lawns and crowding the sidewalks, some nodding and waving at James as he walks by. ØJust checking in, wanted to see how you're doing?Æ Fleamont says easily. Trying to remember how it used to feel to touch him. He falls asleep with his phone in his hand. Part IV Sirius This is going to be a bad day. Always Regulus. ØErîno?Æ he sits down on his bed, shoulders back, spine ramrod straight. Habit. When he hears her voice. Not because he minds being an RA. Not because he doesnít like living on campus. Or because he needs more space. Or privacy or any of the other valid reasons. James wishes he had an apartment because he wishes he had somewhere to bring Sirius. Somewhere where it could just be the two of them. Thatís how theyíve dealt with this stuff before. But he doesnít. And in the end, he decides Siriusís place is better than the dorms. Sirius doesnít talk on the drive back. Itís funny, James thinks, how easily spaces bruise. He can feel it the second he walks into the apartment. The small ache left behind by whatever happened. It hangs in the air, stains the walls, marks the floors. Wincing when you press too hard. He nearly calls out for Remus but that, of course, would be pointless. ØIím just gonna figure out where Moony is yeah?Æ he says to Sirius who isnít really looking at him. Itís not a big apartment. The door to the bathroom is open, so is the door to Remusís room. Both are empty. Sirius would know and it wouldn't do either of them any good. ØMust have left,Æ he says when he walks back into the living room. Sirius hasnít moved. Still shivering. ØHeyîuhîwhy donít you take a shower huh? Warm-up? Iíll order food?Æ It takes a second before Sirius nods. ØYeah, sure.Æ Heís nearly out of the room when James stops him. At his games. In their backyard. Winter would roll around and their father would get out the hose, flooding the whole lawn. Their mother hated it but, for whatever reason, their dad never stopped doing it. The second it was frozen Sirius and Regulus would be out there. The sun would set, and the stars would come out, and neither of them would be able to feel their toes or facesîbut theyíd just keep skating. When he looks for his brother inside himself thatís still where he finds him. Those years when Sirius was too far away to touch, and too hard to call, Regulus would close his eyes and find him skating. He isnít sure why the Sirius in his head still feels closer than the one standing in front of him. Only that heís spent years taking care of the memory of his brother. Keeping him safe. Warm. Loved. All the things he couldnít do for the real Sirius. And maybe thatís the difference. Because the Sirius in his head is the one he didnít lose. He stands at the back of the arena and watches as Sirius does another lap. And then another. And another. Practice is over and everyone else is filing out. James lingers, of course, skates with him for a bit, before eventually following the rest of the guys to the locker room. Regulus presses his back to the cement wall behind him, careful to keep himself hidden. He hasnít been answering Jamesís texts recently. He wants to. He does. But. Well. He canít say the wrong thing if he doesnít say anything, right? Sirius is still doing laps, bag skating himself. He must be exhausted but he just keeps going. Faster. Harder. Taking the corners too sharp. Not for the first time Regulus regrets his decision to come down here. Only heís been staring at his phone for days. A week at this point. Finger hovering over the button unable to call. For some reason this felt easier. When it becomes obvious that Sirius isnít going to be stopping any time soon, Regulus drags himself down to the ice, walking along the rink until he gets to the home team bench. Somehow Sirius still hasnít noticed him. Skating. Skating. Skating. ØSirius!Æ hands shaking slightly, though he tells himself thatís from the cold. ØSirius!Æ Skating. Skating. Skating. Regulus canít help but wonder what heís punishing himself for. ØSIRIUS!Æ The older boyís head turns sharply and for a terrifying moment Regulus thinks heís going to trip, going to go crashing into the ice. He should have known better thoughîSirius skates more than he walks. He catches himself effortlessly, slowing to a stop on the other side of the rink. Just. Staring. At a loss for what to do, Regulus waves at him. Which isèembarrassing. Sirius does not wave back. He does, however, cut across the ice towards him, taking off his helmet as he goes. His hair is soaked with sweat, plastered to his head. ØWhat are you doing here Reg?Æ he asksîchest heaving, words breathless. Regulus knows what heís doing there, he has a whole speech. Heís been going over and over and over it obsessively since he stepped out his front door. Since he woke up that morning. However, standing here, Sirius in front of him, watching, waiting, the idea of saying any of it out loud suddenly feels unbearable. So he shrugs. Shrugs. Sirius huffs out a laugh, shaking his head. ØOkayè?Æ This is not going well. It is, in fact, going very badly. Regulusís eyes dart around, desperate for inspiration. For something to end this silence which is too full. There is so much between them and Regulus can feel it building up, stealing his oxygen, threatening to crush him. Thatís what makes this so hard. Because what can he say, when there are so many years and moments and bruises that never healed? What can he say in the face of all of that? ØYou want water?Æ Regulus manages finally, voice strained. Sirius eyes him for a second before nodding towards the only water bottle still sitting on the bench. Theyíre all just different words for boring.Æ Peter scrunches up his face. ØIím not an English major or anything, but Iím pretty sure thatís not true.Æ Sirius, however, does not appear to be listening. Or perhaps he is simply not interested in the semantics of the English language. ØRemus deserves better than boring. Than nice,Æ he goes on. ØRemus deserves someone incredible. Remusîyou know, he needs someone who sees him. Like properly sees him. Sees how fucking amazing he is. Cause heís likeîyeahîlike the most incredible person I know. And it drives me fucking crazy that heís settling for nice.Æ Siriusís cheeks are pink but Peter canít tell if itís from the speech or the alcohol, his eyes bouncing between Sirius and Fabian. Trying to figure something out. ØWait.Æ His brain whirrs like an overheated engine. ØWait, wait, wait.Æ The other boy sighs. ØI know, okay? I know. Heís allowed to date whomever he wants. Even if the person he wants is fucking lame,Æ Sirius takes a bitter sip of his beer. ØWhat? No.Æ And then, realizing what heís just said: ØI mean yes, obviously Remus can date whoever he wants, but that isnít what IîSirius?Æ he leans forward. ØAre youèare you jealous?Æ There is a moment of absolute stillness, the two boys staring at one another. Peter wonders vaguely if heís about to get punched in the mouth. But thenî Sirius laughs. ØYeah, no shit Iím jealous,Æ itís an obnoxious laugh, like a little kid. ØHeís out here hogging one of my best friends.Æ Peter squints at him, opens his mouth, and then closes it again. ØNah, fuck it. Iím not touching this.Æ ØWhat?Æ Sirius demands as Peter takes another long drag of his drink. ØNot touching what? Whatíre you talking about?Æ ØNothing,Æ Peter wipes his mouth on the back of his hand. ØNothing. Forget aboutîØ at that moment he sees Evan walking back in their direction and drops down flat onto the bar top. Sirius gives him a baffled look. ØWhat the fuck are you doing?Æ ØNothing, shh, Iím not doing anything,Æ he leans to the side of his stool, trying to get as far out of Evanís line of sight as he can. ØSirius justîØ ØOhhh,Æ Sirius interrupts, turning back to Peter with a smug smirk on his face. ØThatís who youíre hiding from,Æ he nods towards Evan who is currently hugging the weird girl that hangs around Regulus. ØWhatever.Æ Sirius bends down so that his face is level with Peterís. ØTalk to him.Æ Peter shakes his head, looking away. And then, after several seconds: ØI donít know what to say.Æ ØThe truth,Æ Sirius responds, like itís simple. Which it isnít. Not at all. Nothing ever is. ØAnd then sorry.Æ ØUgh,Æ Peter scrapes at his face before looking back at Evan. ØI donít know how. IîI donít know how.Æ Sirius seems to think about that for a moment before; ØFor what itís worth, Rosy is a pretty nice guy. I think if youíre looking for understanding heís about as close to it as youíre gonna get.Æ Peter breathes out through his nose. ØYou think I should just do it?Æ Sirius nods while taking a sip of his drink. ØI do.Æ ØNow?Æ ØNo time like the present.Æ Peter groans again, dropping his forehead onto the bar. ØI donít wanna.Æ ØCome on Pete,Æ Sirius slaps him on the back. ØJust get it over with, make James happy yeah? Breathing out and closing his eyes for a second; ØOkay, I can do this,Æ he tries to reassure himself. ØHell yeah you can!Æ Sirius squeezes his shoulder. ØGo forth and prosper or whatever the fuck.Æ Peter cracks his eyes open.ØGood thing James is the one in charge of giving pre-game speeches, huh?Æ ØOh fuck you, Iím very inspirational you little shit,Æ he shoves Peter forward and the goalie giggles, stumbling into the crowd, maybe a little drunker than he should be. Though if heís being honest, there was no world in which he was going to be able to have this conversation sober. Evan is standing against the wall talking to that girl, she leans into him the same way Remus was leaning into Fabian, and he canít help but wonder if theyíre together. Evan spots him when heís still a few feet away, face lighting up as he smiles, making Peterís stomach curdle. ØPettigrew hey!Æ he holds his fist out and Peter meets it. ØHey Rosy, hey,Æ exchanging awkward eye contact with his girl. ØRight, yeah,Æ Evan laughs. ØPeter Cerci, Cerci Peter,Æ gesturing between them. ØHello,Æ Cerci says happily. ØYouíre very good at catching pucks.Æ That makes Peter laugh. ØThank you, I try. You come to our games?Æ She beams at him. ØEvery one.Æ Peter lets out a low whistle. ØDamn, big hockey fan?Æ ØNope,Æ she says, Evan looking at her affectionately. ØBarely know how it works to be honest with you.Æ ØSoèÆ he glances between the pair of them, feeling like heís missed something. Itís not a new feeling for Peter, who is perpetually on the outside of some inside joke. ØWhy come to the games then?Æ ØWell, someone has to wave to Evan.Æ ØRight,Æ like he understands, which he absolutely does not. Heíd ask her to explain but, something about Cerci makes him nervous. ØUmèÆ heís not entirely sure where to go from here, ØwellèÆ the three of them all look at one another, a noticeable lull in the conversation. ØOhîokay, sure dude, whatís up?Æ ØUmèÆ Peter looks at Cerci out of the corner of his eye, passing his weight back and forth between his feet, not sure how to politely ask her to please go away. Iíll go wait for Regulus by the bar.Æ ØSorry,Æ Peter mumbles, feeling incredibly embarrassed by all of this, and wondering if maybe he wouldíve managed it a little smoother without the three beers currently sloshing around in his stomach. In any case, Cerci waves him off. ØNo worries,Æ she closes the already negligible space between her and Evan, going up on her toes and giving him a kiss on the cheekîhe actually still has to lean down slightly for her to reach. Of course.Æ A second later sheís swallowed by the crowd, Evanís eyes following her. ØSoîuhîØ Peter says, trying and failing to sound natural ØAre you two like, a thing then?Æ Evan blinks, refocusing. ØWhat?Æ he seems surprised. ØNo, no, no, weíre justèfriends.Æ Peter gives him a look, ØUh-huh.Æ He wonders if all these people are actually as stupid as theyíre acting, or if theyíre just pretending for some reason. ØYou wanna sit?Æ he asks, gesturing to the tiny table a few feet away. ØSure.Æ Theyíre really too big for thisîthe table wobbles and creaks when they touch it, their knees bumping together when they sit down. Smiling. Him and Fabian should have a nice-off or something. ØWhatís going on?Æ Peter looks at him for a long moment. This is a bad idea. Heís had weeksîweeksîto have this conversation, and heís decided a crowded bar is the place to do it? He should come up with an excuse, make up some other reason he wanted to talk and do this another day. Tomorrow even. Except, the alcohol in his system is saying Øfuck itÆ and for some reason his brain finds that to be a compelling argument. He sighs, running a hand over his face and leaning forward, the table between them threatening to collapse. ØI need to apologize,Æ he says finally. Evan looks at him quizzically but Peter presses on, no longer making eye contact. ØI need to apologize for what happened at the rookie party.Æ ØOh,Æ Evan sounds startled. ØOh well, thatísînah dude, donít worry about it. ØIt was spiked Evan,Æ he forces the words out. ØWe spiked it.Æ He waits. Waits. Waits. But thereís nothing. No shouting. No punches being thrown. Nothing happens. He doesnít know how much time passes exactly, heís not really the best judge at this point, but it feels like a while. Too long. And part of him wonders if Evan has just got up and left, but when he finally forces himself to look up from the table the other boy is still there. Even in the dark he looks pale. ØThe policeîØ Evan has to stop and clear his throat. ØThe police said there were drugs but I thoughtèÆ he breathes out, leaning back slightly. ØI donít remember most of that night so I could have taken something and just forgotten, butèspiked?Æ thereís something trembling about his voice. ØYou guys planned it?Æ It physically pains Peter to speak. ØYeah, weîyeah.Æ Evan just keeps looking at him, so Peter has no choice but to keep going; ØI spiked it.Æ Evanís eyes go wide. ØButîwhat? Weíd barely spoken.Æ ØIt wasnítèpersonal,Æ Peter says, hating the way it sounds. ØWasnít personal,Æ Evan repeats. ØI mean shit Peter, it felt kind of personal.Æ Peter nods, accepting that. ØI know. I know but itîit wasnít supposed to go that far. He wasnít supposed to go that far.Æ ØHeîNick you mean?Æ Peter nods again. ØHe wasnít supposed to make you drink the whole bottle,Æ and then, words scratchy on his tongue. ØHe wasnít supposed to keep feeding you drinks after.Æ ØFeeding me drinks,Æ Evan sounds dazed. ØHe was feeding me drinks?Æ ØYes.Æ ØHe knew the bottle was spiked?Æ ØYes.Æ Evan nods, the movement jerky. ØAnd no one stopped him?Æ Peter canít help but cringe. Itís impossible to explainîexplain that he couldnít. Because you couldnít. You had to play along, if you wanted to be one of the boys you had to play along. ØIím sorry Evan. Really I am, if I could go backîØ ØWhat was it supposed to be like?Æ Evan cuts him off, making Peter fidget. ØAfter you drugged me withîwith what was it?Æ A wave of nausea washes over him. ØRohypnol.Æ Evan laughs again, and even with the noise of the bar Peter can hear the sadness in it. ØYou roofied me? Youîmyîmy team, myîthisîyou roofied me?Æ he scrubs at his face. ØIt wasnít supposed to be the way it was,Æ Peter insists again. ØIt was supposed to be fun Evan, it was supposed to beîØ ØNah but I have a hard time believing that though,Æ his hands drop away from his face, and the remarkable thing is that he doesnít look angry. On me. ØBecause if it was supposed to be fun you would have asked,Æ Evan goes on. ØIf it was supposed to be fun you would have asked. Youîyou wanted me to feel small. The marker on his skin. ØAnd you succeeded,Æ Evan gets clumsily out of his seat. ØScared and small. Thatís exactly how I felt.Æ ØEvanîØ But the other boy waves him off. ØNah Iînot now okay? Iím so fuckingîØ he chokes on his words and Evan just looks back at him blankly. Which Peter thinks is fair because, honestly, what does he expect? ØOkay,Æ is what Evan says eventually. ØIím gonnaîØ he nods in the opposite direction, not waiting for a response before he starts walking away. Itís impossible to lose track of Evan, he towers over most of the room, making his way to the bar. No doubt looking for Cerci and Regulus. Peter hangs his head, hands pressing against his burning face. And for a moment. Just a moment. He hates James. Hates him for forcing this. Maybe telling the truth was the right thing to do, fine. But from where heís sitting, all heís done is hurt them both. Part IV James ØCome on,Æ Fabian is tugging lightly on Remusís arm, smiling shyly at him. ØCome dance with me.Æ ØI canít dance!Æ Remus laughs, before tapping his ear. ØNo rhythm.Æ But fabian is not deterred. ØItís alright, Iím a strong leader.Æ ØOh yeah?Æ They make disgusting eye contact for a good five seconds before Remus eventually shakes his head. ØCanít just leave James here all alone, can I?Æ James snorts. Donít use me as your excuse. Iím not! Remus insists, like a liar. Whatís going on?Æ But Remus only shakes his head. ØIím going to embarrass us both with my terrible dancing, thatís whatís going on.Æ ØHeck yeah!Æ Fabian says excitedly, leaning forward and kissing Remus sweetly before pulling them out of the booth. James smiles, only a little jealous as he looks down at his nearly finished beer, passing the glass back and forth between his hands. Sirius never did come back with that pitcher. He isnít sure where he is. Wouldnít put it past him to have run into some girl on the way to the bar and taken her home. Peter has also disappeared, thatís a bit more of a mystery if heís being honest. He swirls the last of his beer around before draining the glass, putting it down and searching the crowd for his friends. His eyes find their way to the bar, lazily scanning the clusters of faces until a dark head of curls makes his gaze trip and stumble and crash into the floor. For a second he thinks maybe heís having some kind of hallucination, but no. No. Itís definitely him. And almost, probably, most likely real. Regulus. Oh. He must be drunker than he thought because he barely manages to stay in his seat. Oh hello. Oh hi. Oh hey. Youíre here. He swallows with difficulty, eyes glued to the frowning face across the room, occasionally blocked by people walking through Jamesís line of sight. He canít go up to him, he knows he canít, canít do anything but stay right where he is. Because Sirius might still be around. And even if he wasnít, Regulus isèwellèhe wouldnít forgive James for making a scene. And in Regulusís mind the pair of them talking in a bar would count as a scene. So he doesnít move. But he drinks up the sight of the other boy greedily. Always desperate for more. Regulus Black is beautiful. The kind of beauty that makes words twist themselves into knots trying to describe him.That makes the sun bow down in surrender trying to outshine him.That makes Jamesís heart tremble trying to love him. Regulus Black is beautiful. And heís also fucking angry. It takes James a moment to make that observation, partially because Regulus makes his brain go fuzzy, and partially because heís on the other side of a crowded bar. It looks like heís standing with Rosy and Cerciîat least he is until he starts moving. Thereís something about the way he pushes through people, the sharp jerky movements of his arms, the set of his shouldersîlike heís gearing up for a fight. And heís comingètowards James? Which makes no sense at all. James looks around himself, trying to find some plausible explanation but he canít see any other reason that Regulus would be coming in this direction. And heís definitely coming in this direction. The closer he gets the more apparent the rage in his gaze becomes. ØHey,Æ Peter appears out of the crowd. ØSorry that took so long, couldnítîuhîcouldnít find Sirius,Æ James has just enough time to look at Peter before Regulus is there. He grabs Peterís arm yanking him around and punching him in the face. For a second, James swears the whole bar goes still. To Peterís credit, he doesnít hit the ground like Mulciber, but he does stumble back into the table, the empty glasses toppling over and clattering to the floor. James has barely processed any of this when Regulus starts moving again, Peter shoving him back, the pair grappling with one another, staggering around the bar that is far too full for this. Someone is shouting for security. Jamesís brain seems to finally wake up and he launches out of his seat. ØRegulus what the fuck?Æ he shouts as he tries to tear the pair apart. Regulus is the smallest of the three of them, but heís also the most sober, and heís fucking slippery. Arm snaking past James to snag Peterís jaw. ØMan what is your problem!Æ Peter surges forward, managing, despite Jamesís best efforts, to get Regulus by the collar and shake him. ØHey!Æ James shouts, still struggling with them. And then, suddenly, Regulus isnít there anymore. James gets both his hands on Peterís shoulders, holding him back as he looks over to see Sirius struggling with his brother. Heís come at him from behind, one arms across Regulusís chest, the other around his middle, but it is immediately apparent that however drunk James and Peter are, Sirius is much, much drunker. And itís making it hard for him to keep his hold. ØRegulus chill the fuck out!Æ Sirius yells. ØWhat the hell are you doing?Æ ØLet go of me!Æ ØNo, you little psycho.Æ ØLet go of me, fuckingîfuck you! You knew didnít you? You knew and you didnít do anything!Æ James feels Peter go tense under his hands, no longer fighting, and when he looks over at him his face is pale. ØRegulus what are you talking about?Æ ØRegîØ Evan has appeared at the edge of the small space thatís opened up around them, side effect of people trying not to get hit. He looks a little sick, and suddenly the pieces start to fall into place for James. ØCome on, lets just go home yeah?Æ Itís a valiant effort, but there is no way the fury in Regulusís eyes is going to be put out that easily. A fire that wants to burn everything in its path. ØYou fucking knew!Æ ØKnew what Regulus? Christ.Æ The pair of brothers are stumbling around, Sirius unable to keep his balance, Regulus still fighting him. ØHe drugged him.Æ ØRegulus,Æ Evan says weakly. ØLeave it. It doesnít matter.Æ James can now see Cerci at his side, hand on his arm, eyes intent on Regulus, like sheís ready to step in at any moment if she thinks Sirius is being too rough with him. Peter has noticeably wilted, curling in on himself, using James more as a shield than anything else. James lets him go, turning to face Regulus properly. ØItís complicated, okay Regulus?Æ His fury is turned on James now, and while he doesnít buckle under it, itís a near thing. Oh hello. Oh hi. Oh hey. Youíre here. ØComplicated,Æ Regulus spits the word out of his mouth. ØJames Potter, the great moral authority. Everything is black and white with you until one of your buddies is involved right? Then itís complicated.Æ That. Stings. ØIîØ ØThe hell is going on here?Æ says a middle aged man dressed in all black, a walkie-talkie hanging from his belt. No one moves. ØWell?Æ he demands. James thinks the bar might have turned the music off, itís hard to tell, his ears are ringing, everything blurry. Fuck he wishes he was sober. ØNothing,Æ Regulus says finally. ØNothingís going on.Æ The security guard laughs. ØYeah, not buying that kid,Æ eyes bouncing between the lot of them. ØIím leaving. Iím goingîSirius let go of me,Æ he rips himself out of his brotherís hands. ØI started the fight and Iím leaving. So thereís nothing going on.Æ The old man raises his bushy eyebrows. ØNow hold onîØ but Regulus does not, of course, hold on. People practically jumping out of his way as he heads for the door. James and Sirius make bewildered eye contact before James turns to Peter. ØHey,Æ he asks, as quietly as he can over the noise of the room. ØYou okay?Æ Peter wipes the blood from his lip on the back of his hand. ØFine.Æ ØDo you need ice orîØ ØThis is your fucking fault, you know that?Æ James blinks, taking a step back. ØWhat?Æ ØI didnít want to tell him.Æ ØPeterîØ ØI knew this would fucking happen, I knewîØ his voice breaks and James reaches out but Peter shakes his head. ØNo Iîno. I donít want,Æ he exhales. ØIím going home.Æ ØPeter!Æ But Peter doesnít look back. And James isnít sure he should follow. Heís only standing there for a second before: ØJames!Æ He turns around to find Remus making a B-line for him, Fabian a few steps behind, caught in the crowd. ØWhat happened?Æ face lined with concern. ØI couldnít get throughîI couldnítîwhat the hell happened?Æ For a second all James can do is stare at him, not sure he has any idea himself. ØUmîØ he finally manages, voice rough. ØCan you go find Pete?Æ which isnít an answer, but itís what matters right now. ØIîhe doesnít want to see me butîI thinkîumîI think maybe he needs someone.Æ Remus nods slowly. ØYeah, yeah of course,Æ and then, stepping closer. ØJames, are you okay?Æ ØMe?Æ he tries for a reassuring smile. ØSure.Æ Remus looks like heís going to argue but then, apparently, thinks better of it. James is grateful. ØIíll text you when I find him okay?Æ he says, as he starts heading in the direction Peter went. ØThanks.Æ Sirius is gone, so are Rosy and Cerci, it doesnít take a genius to figure out where. James doesnít look for his coat. He just starts moving. Ignoring the looks people give him as he pushes towards the door, the music starting up again just as he makes it outside. The cold feels like having a bucket of ice water dumped over his head, and his teeth start chattering almost instantly. He hadnít realized how hot itíd gotten inside. Without thinking, he starts in the direction of campus. ØThereís no point James.Æ He nearly trips, whirling around himself before he finds Sirius sitting on the ground, back against the bar, head tilted towards the sky. ØWell,Æ Sirius goes on. ØUnless you want to hear some very creative insults. I got åless spine than a jellyfishí and åhockey fuckwití so, thereís really no telling what he might come up with next.Æ James stares at Sirius for a moment before looking back down the street. ØEvan and Cerci are with him?Æ he canít see them, Regulus must have been moving fast. ØYupppp.Æ James wants to go after him anyway. Stupid and reckless and pointless as it would be. He wants to go anyway. Except that, some part of him, the small rational part, knows that it would be a betrayal of Regulus. Putting him in that position. Another betrayal. He sighs, giving up and joining Sirius on the ground. The wall is cold when he leans back against it, the pair of them sitting in silence, listening to the noises of the bar spilling out into the street in small drips. James eventually speaks: ØPeter is mad at me.Æ Sirius snorts. ØWell, isnít that fun of him. Why is that then?Æ James pushes his glasses up into his hair, pinching the bridge of his nose. ØBecause. Because I made him promise to tell Rosy the truth. And Rosy clearly told Regulus ergoîØ Sirius laughs. ØErgo whatíre you writing an essay?Æ ØERGO,Æ James goes on, Øthis is all my fault.Æ ØMm, indeed,Æ Sirius says reasonably. ØHard to argue with that logic.Æ James rolls his head towards him, giving his best friend an exasperated look. ØAre you too drunk for this conversation?Æ ØOn the contrary, Iím not nearly drunk enough.Æ Now itís Jamesís turn to laugh, knocking their shoulders together as they drift into silence again, the night interrupted by the occasional car, or drunk college student stumbling by. ØI donít thinkèI donít think your friend dating someone is supposed to feel like this.Æ Sirius is still staring very resolutely across the street, face blank, every muscle in his body held tight. ØI know.Æ Like it hurts. James isnít sure what to do with that. Isnít sure what he was expecting. Feelings are not straightforward things with Sirius. They never have been. ØRegulus. Remus. I keep getting it wrong.Æ James only shakes his head, still holding Sirius against him as he looks up at the stars. ØWeíre gonna be okay, you know?Æ he manages finally. Heís not even sure where it comes from exactlyîvague memories of sleepy promises made into Regulusís neck. Iíd find you. In every universe. You and Sirius. Iíd find you and keep you safe. James squeezes Sirius a little tighter. ØWeíre going to be okay.Æ In every universe. Iíll make sure of it. Chapter End Notes Heeeeeeeyyyyyyyyyyy! I love the idea that all fanfiction is like an alternative universe in a way, and that in every reality these characters find and love each other, and so like, James in this chapter was a bit of a nod to that, a breaking of the fourth wall if you will Also very big fan of Regulus's "Nobody fucks with my baby" attitude when it comes to Evan, just forever defending his honour I would like to say more things but I am very tired and my brain has turned into some kind of sludge-like substance that is just slopping around in my skull (ew) BUT other chapters will definitely follow this chapter and things will for sure be happing in them I can guarantee it! LOVE YOU BYE woooooooow I totally forgot about the French (sorry France, love you promise, real long bread, very smelly cheese, so mean, I'm into it) "Why are you defending her? I don't understand why you left if you're going to continue being her fucking lapdog." Chapter14 Chapter Summary Oh brother Chapter Notes tw homophobic language (Mulciber) tw the like smallest bit of violence tw eating stuff, just, mostly the effects of not eating, some anxiety around eating, weight loss is mentioned (like reg has lost weight that's the extent there's no detailed descriptions of it) See the end of the chapter for more notes Part I Regulus He stares at the words on the page in front of him and begs his mind to focus. Please, please, please. But he keeps getting down to the bottom of the page without any memory of what came before. His eyes flick to the time in the corner of his screen. Itís already been an hour. This is only the first reading. He has about another seven he has to get through before tomorrow. Please, please, please. His fingers drum on the desk. His leg bouncing up and down underneath. Head held between his hands. Eyes on the floor. Trying to stop the room from spinning. He doesnít feel good. He doesnít think itís the alcohol either, but he pretends it is anyway. This is pathetic. Heís pathetic. Which isnít something heís used to. Heís not sure why heís all twisted up about Regulus. Why he seems to need him so much, when heís never needed anyone before. Usually people need James. Heíd thought he understood Regulus. That Regulus was justîthat he was scared. That this was a lot. That he was worried about Sirius. About his parents. But that, despite all of that, he still felt what James feels. This ache. This want. This hunger. He knows Regulus is shit at expressing it. ØPaperwork doesnít get processed in time so the funding is released late.Æ Severus feels like heís going to be sick. Or break something. Itís hard to tell which. He had an inconvenient habit of ignoring Severusís calls. ØYes well,Æ the woman goes on in the same airy tone. ØYou really need to get these sorts of things in early.Æ Break something. Surely. He doubts very much that James Potter or Sirius Black have ever worried about money in their lives. And thatís just from what she sees. She cannot imagine what is happening in all the places she isnít. She tells Crouch Sr., heís the coach after all, she hopes heíll talk to them. Look out for them. From the coaches to the caretakers, people fall all over themselves in front of him. He smiles, he says hello to everyone, has impeccable manners for a twenty-year old boy. ØMm,Æ the younger boy answers, which doesnít exactly inspire confidence in James. ØYou need to stop?Æ ØNo, I can make it to the car. Just. Stop. Talking.Æ James doesnít argue. Opening the door for him when they finally get there and helping him into the passenger seat. Regulus instantly curls forward, dropping his head between his knees again. James canít help reaching out, putting his hand on his back, and rubbing slow circles into it. ØRegîØ ØIím fine. Can you justîØ but he cuts himself off. Itís a few seconds before his voice comes back, thin and tense: ØI just want to go home.Æ James nods, ØOkay,Æ eventually forcing himself to pull away and get into his own side of the car. He gives Regulus one last look before pulling away from the curb and into the street. It takes a few minutes before Regulus sits up, leaning his head back against the seat. ØSorry,Æ he says again, eyes straight ahead. James shoots him a look out of the corner of his eye. ØWhy?Æ ØJustèÆ Regulus pulls his mouth into an unhappy line. ØSeverus shouldnít have called you. But he wouldnít leave me alone and IîI didnít know what else to do. I didnít want him to call a fucking ambulance. Or Sirius.Æ James can see the bruise now, big and blue, near Regulusís temple, above his left eye. ØWhy didnít you want him to call Sirius?Æ James asks finally. Regulus lets out a long breath. ØHeíd justèfreak out.Æ James almost laughs. Because he is definitely freaking out. He honestly might be past freaking out at this point, moved up to some more serious level of worry. Regulus rolls his head in Jamesís direction. ØDonít tell him about this okay?Æ he says, and James grimaces. More secrets. He fucking hates secrets. He wasnít built for them, they donít fit right inside his skin. ØWhy?Æ he asks, making a lefthand turn. ØLike I said, heíll just freak out,Æ and then, when James doesnít say anything. ØThis isnít a big deal. Just blood sugar or something. Iíll be fine. I am fine.Æ James doesnít think thatís quite true. ØWe should get you something to eat,Æ he says instead. Regulus only hums noncommittally, looking out the window. ØI just want to sleep,Æ he says finally, and James doesnít know what to do. Something is wrong. Something is clearly wrong. Heíd thought maybe it was just stress, first year is always hardîadjusting, figuring out new routines. But thisèit feels like more. ØReg, you knowèÆ he isnít sure what he wants to say. ØYou can talk to me right?Æ ØI am talking to you.Æ ØRight butèyou can talk to meèabout stuff.Æ He can feel the tension wafting off of Regulus without even looking at him. ØStuff?Æ he asks brittlely. ØYeah,Æ James pushes on, uncertain. ØIím here forîwhatever you need okay? I really donít mind. Whatever it is, you wonít scare me away. Thatís what Iím trying to tell you.Æ Regulus doesnít speak for a long time, James shooting nervous looks in his direction periodically. Eventually the younger boy lets out a deep sigh, the kind that seems to come from his whole body. ØThereís nothing to tell James,Æ he says. ØI promise. Iím fine.Æ Which is the first moment it hits James. Properly hits him. That Sirius isnít the only person Regulus is keeping secrets from. Part III Evan Itís the first thing he thinks when he wakes up. He doesnít even know why. But itís right there, floating above all the other thoughts. Impossible to ignore. Not that heís trying to ignore it. At least, he doesnít think he is? He just wishes it wasnít soèbig. Wishes it was just another thing he knew. Wishes that he could feel casual about it. But he canít. Today is Bartyís Birthday. At first he decides that he wonít say anything. Things have gone back to the way they were last summer. No speaking. No contact. And itís for the best. Healthy. So heís not going to say anything. Barty probably doesnít want to hear from him anyway. That plan lasts until about noon. Because, well, heís not a monster. Itís Bartyís birthday for goodnessí sake! He canít say nothing. That would just be rude. So heíll send him a text. Casual. Generic. Something he would get from any one of his old teammates. Thatís okay right? You can move on without cutting someone off completely. Surely a birthday text wonít be a big deal? He spends the next few hours crafting the message. Capitals or no capitals? Exclamation marks or no exclamation marks? Emojis? Gifs? Should he say: hope youíre having a good birthday! or just happy birthday! Which is more casual? The second one right? Fewer words are probably better. Probably more casual. Itís nearly five in the evening when he gives up. When he leans against the side of his residence building, hand freezing because he forgot his gloves, phone pressed to his ear. Calling before he can change his mind. At first he thinks Barty isnít going to pick-up. But just when Evan starts scrambling to come-up with some kind of voice message to leave he hearsî ØHello?Æ His whole body reacts to that voice. Like being struck by lightening. Like having a hand shoved through his ribs. His throat tight. ØHappy Birthday,Æ he manages, cringing at how high his voice sounds. How clearly uncomfortable he is. Thereís a pause on the other end of the phone, and then: ØYou know, youíre the second person to say that to me today?Æ He doesnít sound sad about it. Doesnít sound anything at all about it really, like itís just an observation heís making. Still, something twists painfully in Evanís chest. Only the second? is all he can think. ØOh yeah?Æ he manages to choke out. ØOur new trainer, Alice, said happy birthday to me this morning. Scared the shit out of me honestly, I had no idea how she knew. Itís on my medical forms I guess.Æ ØRight, yeah, that makes sense,Æ Evan is nodding even though Barty canít see him. Trying not to let himself really take in that the only person in Bartyís life to wish him a happy birthday was his schoolís physiotherapist. ØSo,Æ he finally manages. ØGood day?Æ ØItís just a day Evan.Æ ØNah come on, donít say that,Æ he brings his free hand up to press down on his sternum, like he can keep the emotions from crawling up his throat. ØItís your birthday.Æ More silence. And then: ØYou remember last year?Æ Evan squeezes his eyes shut. ØYeah.Æ ØYou said it was too cold to picnic outside, so you laid a blanket down in your basement, and you had a basket and everything, we ate on the floor.Æ ØYeah,Æ he says again, clearing his throat. ØLame, I know.Æ He doesnít mean that. Heíd loved that day. Barty had smiled so big when heíd seen the picnic basket. The food inside. The pathetically decorated cupcakes Evan had suffocated in sprinkles. You made these for me? Barty had asked. Of course. Of course, Barty repeated, like he couldnít quite believe it. ØI liked it,Æ his voice crackles over the line now. And Evanís chest squeezes. ØYeah,Æ he manages finally. ØYeah, I liked it too.Æ For a minute they just stay like that, neither of them speaking, Evan should probably hang up. Heíd wanted to wish him a happy birthday and now he has so. He should put them both out of their misery. But he canít. He never can with Barty. Eventually he leans his head back against the wall, sighing. ØThis is so fucked up.Æ Barty doesnít answer right away. When his voice does come itís quiet. ØYeah.Æ ØI need to stop calling you.Æ ØOkay.Æ He doesnít fight it, he rarely does. ØI need to stop wanting to call you.Æ ØWhat do you want me to say Evan?Æ Evan shakes his head, laughing without humour. ØThe same thing I always want you to say.Æ ØWhich is?Æ ØThat youíll stop playing. That youíll get away from your dad. That youíllîØ be with me. Be with me. Be with me. ØIím not going to say any of that.Æ Another empty laugh. ØI know. I know youíre not. Thatís why I need to stop.Æ ØSo why donít you?Æ Evan shakes his head. ØI donít know,Æ and then. ØWhy donít you?Æ Silence. ØI donít know.Æ ØWell then.Æ ØYeah.Æ Evan exhales. ØRight. Okay. I hopeîuhîI hope you have a good rest of your day.Æ Barty doesnít respond. ØIíllîuhîIíll see you, I guess.Æ ØAt the championships.Æ ØProbably, yeah.Æ ØIím not going to go easy on you.Æ Evan canít help but smile. ØI know you wonít Bee. You never do.Æ He can hear Bartyís breath catch on the other end. ØYouîØ Bartyís voice cracks, and then Evan hears him exhale. He wants to apologize but he doesnít even know what for. Whatever it is he did to make Barty sound like that. Like someoneís just checked him into the boards. ØI?Æ Evan asks after a few seconds. ØNothing.Æ ØBeeîØ ØStop.Æ ØIím sorry.Æ ØI hate that fucking nickname.Æ Evan tries not to flinch, but when he speaks his voice is small. ØOkay.Æ ØJust call me Crouch like everyone else.Æ ØAlright.Æ Tense silence. The type that creeps along your bones. That turns your stomach. ØI have to go.Æ ØRight.Æ ØBye.Æ ØByîØ but the word isnít even fully out of Evanís mouth before the line goes dead. Chapter End Notes HELLO! I know this chapter was a bit short and sweet but SURPRISE the next one is coming out tomorrow so hopefully that makes up for it! Wolfstar getting their shit together! adore them! stunning! love to see it! so excited for them to not betray each other and then end up in jail falsely accused of murder and then tragically die like two years apart :) :) :) :):):):)):))) Jegulus NOT doing so hot, things are deteriorating. Sheís wearing gym shorts. Her hair fanned out around her. She lies on her side facing Mary, who mirrors her position. The bed is warm and their foreheads are nearly touching. All of them is nearly touching. Mary has the desire to close the space between them but she doesnít. The morning is soft. Lily wrinkling her nose as she squints her eyes open. There is mascara smudged on the tops of her cheeks, red lines crisscrossing her face from the blankets. She is so beautiful it hurts. ØHi,Æ she croaks, smiling. ØHi,Æ Mary says back. ØWere you watching me sleep?Æ Yes. ØYour snoring woke me up.Æ Lily gasps, offended. ØI do not snore!Æ ØYou do. I know you will. And thatís what scares her. Lily loves like she never learned what ribcages are for. Like everyone ought to be allowed inside. Lily doesnít wear her heart on her sleeve, but carries it around in the palm of her hand. Giving it to anyone who asks. Lily will see this through to the end, just like she did with James. Mary isnít so sure about herself. As a rule, she doesnít finish things. Or maybe thatís not right, maybe she just doesnít start them. Dismissing them before they even begin. Itís easier that way. Cleaner. A second later Lily is rolling Mary onto her back, climbing on top of herîthough probably not as smoothly as sheíd like to, getting tangled in the bed sheets in the process and spending several seconds trying to get herself free. ØGotchu,Æ she says eventually, breathless and rosy cheeked. Mary looks up at her blankly. ØCongratulations.Æ Lily rolls her eyes. ØYouíre no fun.Æ ØIf you say so.Æ She places her hands on either side of Maryís head, lowering herself slightly. ØProve me wrong.Æ Mary does her best, she really does, but she ends up looking at Lilyís mouth. The other girlís lips curling up in a satisfied smirk. ØIíve got work Evans.Æ ØMhm.Æ ØI do.Æ ØAlright.Æ Mary doesnít understand how their faces have gotten closer together, noses brushing. Not that it matters. Sheís weak. Always has been for Lily. Mary reaches up, closing the last few inches between them and kissing her. The other other girl makes a satisfied hum, sinking into it. But Mary doesnít let it go on too long, bucking her hips after a few seconds and knocking Lily off. She makes an indignant squawk as Mary sits up, swinging her legs over the side of the mattress. ØI really do have to go to work.Æ Lily sighs dramatically. ØFine.Æ Mary looks over at her, sprawled on her back, and she canít resist reaching out and pinching her. ØHey! Ow!Æ Lily giggles, and when her bright green eyes find Maryís she smiles back despite herself, if only for a moment. And then her phone starts buzzing. ØChrist, did you set a third alarm? Youíre way more neurotic than I thought.Æ The first alarm had been for prayer but Mary doesnít bother pointing that out, too busy staring at her phone. ØThatís notîØ ØYou know, I carried you around for nine months? Nine whole months.Æ ØYou donít say.Æ ØLeast you could do is spare a few minutes toîØ ØYes, alright, okay. Jeez.Æ ØJeez,Æ her mother says teasingly, Mary doesnít need to be able to see her face to know sheís smiling. Her mother is always smiling. ØHow is work then? ØSo are you going to shower orè?Æ ØYeah,Æ fuck. ØYeah. See youèlater, I guess?Æ ØLater,Æ Lily repeats like she's never heard the word before, and Mary nods, leaving the room before either of them can make this anymore awkward. Her father died when she was young. Her brother remembers him. Mary doesnít. So she grew up in a house full of women. Full of aunts. And cousins. And grandmothers. The door was always open and women in beautiful clothes, smelling of beautiful perfumes, and carrying beautiful food, were always coming in. And always ending up in the kitchen. Chopping and frying and baking. Mary used to sit there and watch as they rolled out the dough for the manaeesh, or simmered shushbarak on the stove. Sit there and listen while they yelled at their soap operas on the tv or sang along to Fairuz on the radio. It was so bright, their kitchen, with all those women and their colourful veils, flitting about like butterflies. Laughing and touching and eating with one another. Itís one of the reasons she has always liked living with Marlene and Lily. One of the reasons she didnít mind when Dorcas more or less unofficially moved in. When theyíre all home, singing in the kitchenîteasing each other over boiling pots, and sizzling skilletsîit feels like family to Mary. Love has always been a taste to her more than anything else. Something warm and full of garlic. Something given to you by familiar hands. Something shared together at the table. Elbows brushing. Mary loves her family. Loves her mother. She doesnít think they have much in common, but she loves her anyway. Loves her too much to be honest. The first time Mary kissed a girl she was fifteen. It was a birthday party. One of her friends, Jessica B., pulled her into the washroom in the basement and kissed her. When Mary returned home and her mother asked her about the party she didnít mention it. She has never mentioned any of the girls sheís kissed to her mother. Her mother who smiles all the time. Who calls at least once a day. She does not tell her mother because she loves her mother. And she is fairly certain this is something her mother doesnít want to know. So she keeps her life separated into neat, self-contained boxes. Because she would rather have nothing in common with her mother. Than not have her at all. I can give you all the hope in the world, grandmother said to me. But without faith itís only a mirage in a desert youíre lost in. ØMorning,Æ Fabian says sleepily, waiting for her by the locked doors to the Galley. Itís time to stopèÆ she waves her hand in his general direction, Øpouting.Æ This does nothing to make Fabian look any less confused. Mary sighs, pinching the bridge of her nose. ØYouíre not this pathetic Fabian so stop acting like it.Æ His cheeks instantly go pink, eyes dropping to the ground. But spending every day coming up with a new sad poem to write in your diary isnít helping.Æ Thereís a moment of silence, Fabian shuffling his feet, toying with the empty cup in his hand. You kind of have to figure that out yourself.Æ ØBut how?Æ his eyes flick up and then away. ØI keep waiting for it to fade, you know?Æ he brings his free hand up to his chest, tapping it almost absentmindedly. I know I shouldnít, we werenít even together that long butèhe was so big. And I made so much space for him. I donít know how to fill those spaces now.Æ Oh. Well then. Mary eyes him warily. If he starts crying she might have to fire him. Or herself. One or the other. She really should have waited to do this until Lily was here. Sheís better at the touchy.feely stuff. ØLook, itís not a switch,Æ Mary manages finally. ØYour feelings arenít just going to suddenly turn off,Æ though wouldnít that be nice? ØYou have to decide to move on, to fake it for a bit, and then eventually, one day, youíll wake up and realize itís been a long time since you last thought about him. But you canít just sit around waiting for that to happen on its own because it wonít. Youíll just start toèrot.Æ Mary would know. Sheís been rotting for years. Watching Lily and James, so pretty and perfect together. So soft and sweet. It was sickening. It was beautiful. In a twisted sort of way sheíd wanted them to be together just as much as everyone else. Sheíd never seen a real life fairytale before. Sometimes it felt worth it, even if it did make her want to break her ribs one by one. Fabian is nodding his head, but in a jerky sort of way that still means theyíre in dangerous crying territory. Heartbroken?Æ Mary shakes her head. ØFeel like you donít want to let them go.Æ And when he keeps looking at her with those stupid eyes: ØYou donít just get one shot Fabian.
