ØItís fine dude. I swear. Just a laugh, a rite of passage, yeah? Youíll see, next year, youíll see.Æ Next year. When James is the one spitting in the cup. Thatís what he means. James isnít sure he likes the sound of that. He feels uncertain though, he doesnít know what to do here. Everything a bit blurry. He watches them write insults all over Peterís naked torso. He tries to tell himself that they must know more than him, must know that Peter is okay. They wouldnít actually hurt him, right? He isn't sure how much time passes. It feels like a lot. His heart jack hammering in his chest the whole time, telling him this is wrong, even while everyone else tells him it isnít. He feels hot, a sick kind of sweat sticking to his skin. Peter doesnít move the whole time. It doesnít feel right. It doesnít feel normal. Heís not sure what to do. ØHey, weíre running out of space here!Æ someone calls out. Theyíre talking about Peter. Theyíre running out of room on Peter. Jamesís stomach lurches. ØTake off his pants!Æ Someone else yells back. This gets applause. Whoops. The guy crouched in front of Peter laughs, shaking his head as he sticks the end of the marker in his mouth and reaches for the button on Peterís jeans. And for whatever reason, thatís the last straw for James. He pushes away from the wall, past Mac who has stopped paying attention to him. ØEnough,Æ he says, making his way up to the chair. ØEnough!Æ more forcibly shoving the guy currently trying to strip his friend out of the way. He can feel the tension in the room, the eyes on him, the whispering. ØSeriously Potter? Canít you take a fucking joke?Æ James ignores him, pulling Peterís pants back into place. He doesnít know where his shirtís gone. ØDude, lighten up,Æ the guy tries to grab at him but then Sirius is there, shoving him back to the ground. ØDonít fucking touch him,Æ Sirius snaps. James is barely paying attention as people shout at him, at Sirius. He awkwardly gets himself under Peterís arm, hauling him over his shoulder and standing up with difficulty. There are shaking heads, and rolling eyes, but no one tries to stop them again. Not even Mac. Maybe theyíve finally gotten bored with this, James doesnít care, doesnít stop to ask questions. He feels overwhelmed by shame but he doesnít know what for. For letting this happen? For not letting it happen? They go back to the residence building because James doesnít know where else to go. Heís on his way to Peterís room when Sirius grabs him by the elbow and tugs him into the bathroom. ØHe needs to throw-up.Æ ØHeís unconscious.Æ ØJustîhereîbring him here.Æ They end up in one of the stalls, holding Peter up over the toilet, Sirius with his fingers down his throat. Itís awful. Itís awful. This horrible rotting sick feeling in Jamesís stomach. Fear. And the desire to call his mom. To have an adult to tell him what to do. To take charge. To take responsibility. To promise that things will be okay. James really doesnít think itís going to work. But eventually, it does. Peterís sick. Heís sick for hours. Coming in and out of consciousness. At some point Sirius falls asleep. But James never does. Sits there with his hand on his friendís back feeling like he has no idea who he is. But knowing who he doesnít want to be. Not ever. He wonít be Mac. He wonít. He wonít ever make anyone else feel this fucking small. James Present Day The room is silent. None of the three boys talking. Moving. ØPeterîØ ØIt wasnít your fault.Æ ØI should have done more.Æ Peter shakes his head, hand ghosting over his chest, the places they marked his skin. ØItís okay. You did your best. ØI donít know exactlyèI thoughtîI thought it would make me feel less scared, maybe? Itís like thereís thisèdread, you know? Every time Iím in that locker room. Like Iím afraid to take my shirt off. I used to fucking love the locker room, the boys, shooting the shit,Æ he shakes his head. ØI thought it would help. It didnít. Even if it had, I shouldnít have done it.Æ The problem with people. With being human. Is that sometimes, you do the wrong thing. Sometimes you hurt. And you canít take it back. And you canít make it better. You just have to keep living. You just have to wake up every morning with the heavy knowledge that you had a choice and you chose wrong. And maybe you try to make amends. Or you try to forget. Or you try to set it on fire. But one way or another youíll have to figure out how to live with it. That truth. That inescapable reality. James knows that. He gets it. He stood in the middle of a party with his captainís hand on his shoulder and did the wrong thing. ØOkay,Æ he manages finally. Like the weight of buildings. Empires. Have been lifted from his shoulders. ØVite Vite, lets go, bring it in.Æ James manages a choked kind of laugh, him and Peter both getting up, the three of them wrapping their arms around each other. Itís like theyíre on the ice. Sirius has his helmet on, looking ready to kill someone as he takes his place. Eventually, painfully, Regulus shakes his head. ØWe canít leave without him.Æ Remus gives him a weak smile, the fear clear in his eyes. ØNo,Æ he says finally. ØNo we canít.Æ It is immediately apparent that something is wrong as soon as the puck drops. Regulus had no idea why Moody put Sirius back out on the ice. At best heíd be distracted, at worst heíd do something reckless in retaliation. But Regulus was wrong. Because it isnít Sirius who goes off the rails. Itís Evan. ØWoah,Æ Cerci says beside him. ØWhat is heîØ But before sheís able to finish that sentence it becomes painfully clear what Evan is doing. Riddle doesnít have the puck. But then, neither had James so, fairís fair Regulus supposes. Evan slams into Riddle, the other boy hitting the boards before crumpling to his knees on the ice. ØOh fuck,Æ Cerci hisses beside him, hands coming up to cover her mouth. Evan is standing over Riddle, Regulus thinks he might be shouting at him but itís hard to tell from this angle. The linesmen are moving towards them, no doubt ready to pull Evan off. He doubts Evan will see any ice for the rest of the game. But then. Someone gets to him first. Lestrange crosschecks Evan in the back, sending him onto the ice right along with Riddle. Cerci makes a distressed noise beside him as Lestrange raises his stick again. Looking very much like heís about to bring it down on Evanís neck. ØNo,Æ Regulus thinks he says, though itís so hard to tell, the arena has irrupted around him and he can barely hear himself think. He canít watch this. He canít watchî A fourth body collides with Lestrange, knocking the stick out of his hands and throwing him into the glass. Barty Crouch takes his teammateís jersey in his hands and gives him a vicious shake. The pair grappling with one another. Pushing and pullingîhelmets come off. Gloves. Lestrange tries to throw a punch but it barely seems to touch Crouch, who returns the hit tenfold. Again. And again. And Again. Regulus thinks Lestrange might have lost some teeth after the third one. He watches Evan, still on the ice, reaching out, like heís trying to stop Barty, but before he can Riddle swipes at him with his skate blade. And a second later the two of them are going at it, rolling around on the ice. ØWhere the fuck are the refs?Æ Regulus demands, all of this happening so quickly he can barely follow, his thoughts running a mile a minute, heart pounding in his chest. ØUm,Æ Cerci says nervously from beside him. ØI think theyíre busy.Æ ØWhat?Æ he looks at her and then follows her gaze to the other end of the rink, which is when Regulus realizes that both teams have jumped the boards and stormed the ice, creating a massive, bench clearing brawl. ØHoly shit,Æ Regulus watches the chaos wide-eyed. ØIs this normal for hockey?Æ Cerci asks, sounding slightly panicked. There is something undeniably frightening about the violence, about he way itís bleeding into the crowd that was already tense after Jamesís injury. Things are being thrown onto the ice, people shouting at one another in their seats. The linesmen and refs are doing their best to take control of the situation on the ice but theyíre pathetically out numbered. ØGuys,Æ Regulus thinks itís Dorcas who speaks but heís not sure, unable to take his eyes off the scene unfolding in front of him. ØI think we should get out of here, this is starting to feel like the beginning of a fucking riot.Æ Regulusís eyes scan the fighting, looking for Sirius. It takes a second, but eventually he spots him. Going at it with one of his own teammatesîMulciber. He watches them throw each other around, Mulciber kneeing Sirius in the stomach, making him double over, choking. A sharp flash of fury cuts through Regulus. And before he knows what heís doing heís standing. ØRegulus?Æ Cerci asks, but he pushes past her, out onto the stairs. ØRegulus!Æ thatís Remus, but he doesnít acknowledge him either. Itís chaos. Some people clearly trying to leave, dozens more pushing towards the glass with their phones outstretched, filming all of this. No doubt itíll be viral before they even get out of the arena. He manages to move towards the tunnel that leads to the Griffinís locker room. Thereís a barrier between it and the stands, Regulus pushes his way down as close to ice level as he can get, amidst all the action no one seems to be paying much attention to who is supposed to be where. He doesnít let himself think, placing a hand on the top of the metal barrier and vaulting himself over. Falling eight or ten feet. He lands on his hands and knees, pain shooting up his shins as he half crawls and half stumbles back up. The people whoíd seen him shout, but he doesnít listen, doesnít look back, pushing himself forward and towards Godricís bench, now empty, with all its players on the ice. If everyone werenít watching the fighting he doesnít think he would have gotten away with this, but as it is, no one seems to notice what heís doing until heís stepping onto the ice. ØHey! Who the fuck is that kid?!Æ he thinks he hears Moody shouting behind him. His feet nearly fly out from under him the second he touches the rink, luckily heís ready for it. He spent his childhood on the ice, he knows how it feels under his running shoes. He shortens his steps, half-jogging, half-shuffling his way across the ice. Itís absurd really, him skidding between wrestling hockey players. All of this is fucking absurd. But he doesnít stop, doesnít turn around, Mulciberís back coming into view as Regulus just narrowly avoids being taken down by a flailing hockey stick. Mulciber and Sirius have each other by their jerseys, trading punches, neither of them able to get a good enough grip to really land anything substantial. Mulciber, who is already taller than Regulus, has a significant advantage now that heís in his skates. So really, given his relatively shaky balance at the moment, and the current height difference, Regulus figures just about the only thing he has going for him is the element of surprise. Like jumping over the barrier, he doesnít give himself the chance to think. To consider all the ways this could go wrong. All the reasons it shouldnít work. The minute Mulciber is within reach Regulus practically leaps onto his back, getting his arm around Mulciberís neck in something vaguely like a chokehold. A strangled noise comes out of Mulciber, and he lets go of Sirius, but Regulus doesnít have much time to feel good about that, because it becomes immediately apparent that Regulusís surprise attack has caused Mulciber to lose his balance, and a second later Regulus finds himself slamming into the ice, all two-hundred pounds of Nicolai Mulciber landing on top of him. The air is punched out of his lungs, his vision going blurry, making it suddenly hard to tell which way is up or down. He feels the mountainous weight lift off of him as heís still trying to blink the world back into focus. He knows thatís not good. Knows it means Mulciber has recovered and is, probably any second, going to start beating the absolute shit out of him. He tries to make himself move, even just to curl into the fetal position, but his body doesnít seem to be back online yet. The first solid thing heís able to focus on is a hand. He flinches at first, assuming its aim is to cause him some sort of bodily harm. But then it wiggles its fingers, and Regulus, squinting, is able to follow those fingers to their wrist, arm, shoulder, neck, and finallyî ØCome on Reg,Æ Sirius says, hovering above him. ØUp you get, lets go.Æ Regulus blinks a few more times before, finally, managing to move his arm. To take Siriusís hand, groaning as his older brother pulls him to his feet, holding Regulusís side to steady him. ØYou alright?Æ Regulus nods, head swimming a bit, but otherwise he thinks he might actually be okay. When he meets his brotherís eyes Sirius grins at him, mouthguard lost, teeth covered in blood. ØYouíre on the ice.Æ Regulus huffs. ØIím on the ice,Æ and then: ØMulciber?Æ Siriusís grin only widens, nodding his head to the ground beside them where Regulus finds Mulciber flat out on his back. ØYou softened him up for me,Æ Sirius says cheekily. ØRight,Æ Regulus thinks heís about to say something else when an amplified voice cuts across the rink. ØAlright, everyone put your hands up now, in the air, lets see them!Æ Siriusís eyes go over Regulusís head and grow wide, a second later Regulus sees why. Uniformed police officers are making their way onto the ice, grabbing hockey players and hauling them off. ØOh,Æ Regulus watches in mild panic. ØOh fuck.Æ Barty His jaw is sore. So are his fists. The cops ask him a few questionsîmostly his name, contact information. There are too many people and too much going on for them to really properly interview anyone. Or charge anyone for that matter. But there is the very clear implication that he will be contacted later. He doesnít care. Doesnít care if heís charged. Fined. If he has to do community service. Heíd do it again. Has half a mind to hunt down Lestrange and get in a few more hits now. Get his hands around his neck. Fucking kill him. Thatís what his plan had been, if you could call it a plan. He saw him knock Evan down, and then he saw him raise his stick. And all he could think was: I will tear you apart. I will break your bones. I will eat you. He doesnít hunt down Lestrange, however. Still half in his gear, he starts making his way through the chaotic arena towards Godricís locker room. He does make sure to take his jersey off first howeverîno need to advertise that heís the enemy. There are some parents and family members standing around near the mouth of the hallway, scolding their sons or cradling their injuries. More cops too, of course. Barty searches the crowd for Evanîusually not that difficult a person to find given what a fucking giant he is. Barty doesnít know how badly Evan was hurt, thatís the thing thatís making something with claws tear up his chest. They didnít have time to talk. There was fighting and then there were strong arms and men in uniform and yelling and Evan was being dragged in the opposite direction. He tries to move his way closer to the locker room, but the small hallway is packed and itís hard to get anywhere. Heís still a good few paces away from the door when he hears someone calling his name. ØBarty!Æ He turns his head just in time to see Cerci running towards him, her arms immediately wrapping around him, holding him tight, face buried in his chest. For a second he has no idea what to do, feeling a little stunned, before finally managing to hug her back. ØUh, hi.Æ ØHow are you? Are you okay?Æ she pulls back, hands going to his bruised face. All he can do is blink down at her. ØYouíre a superhero you know that? When I saw that asshole lift his stick IîØ her voice breaks and she shakes her head. Like she doesnít even have the words for that fear. Barty understands. Heíd felt it too. ØBut then there you were,Æ she smiles at him, not the bright, sparkly one heís been on the receiving end of before, but softer. Rawer. ØAre you sure youíre okay?Æ she asks after a small pause. ØErîØ and then, clearing his throat. ØYeahîyeah, he didnít really get me much.Æ ØYou kicked his ass.Æ That startles a laugh out of him. ØI tried.Æ She finally lets him go, as if only just realizing she was still holding him. ØIím glad youíre okay.Æ And the thing he canít understand, is how much it feels like she means it. He still finds it difficult to wrap his mind around the fact that Cerci doesnít hate him. He thinks that she should. Heís inexplicably happy that she doesnít. ØThanks,Æ he finally manages. Unsure of what else to say. She smiles at him and thenî ØBee?Æ Barty whips around. Evan is standing in front of the locker room, hastily applied bandage over his already purple left eye. A second later theyíre colliding, arms wrapping around one another. ØAre you alright?Æ he tries to pull back, to get a better look at Evan, but Evan doesnít appear to be a fan of that idea, arms squeezing him tighter. ØThat crosscheck looked brutal, IîØ but he doesnít get to finish, Evanís hands coming up to Bartyís face before he kisses him. In the arena. Hallway full of people. Full of all the wrong kinds of people to be doing this in front of. And he should shove him off. Should insist they go somewhere even a little bit private. But he doesnít. Instead he lets himself go soft in Evanís arms. Lets himself breathe. Heís okay. Heís okay. Heís okay. Eventually Evan pulls back, pressing their foreheads together for a moment before letting go completely. ØSome bruising,Æ he says, and Barty blinks, trying to focus. ØWhat?Æ Evan smiles. ØI didnít mean to scare you.Æ ØWell, if youíre sorry thatís alright then.Æ Evan leans down, kissing the top of her head. And Barty really feels like he should not be here, but then Cerci goes on. Heís awkward and stiff, but itís been weeks since someone has touched him like this. Since he left Evan standing in that parking lot. And so even though it goes against every natural instinct in his body he lets them crowd him. Lets them squeeze and tug and kiss him. His head. His cheeks. His temples. Now see? Was that so hard? Letting me love you. Still tangled up, Barty hears Evan sigh. ØFor James.Æ Regulus Remus is waiting for them when they get done with the police, Sirius barely dressedîbutton up hanging open, jacket and tie stuffed in his bag. At least his pants are done up. ØOkay.Æ Hospitals are always much more complicated than people think they are. They are full of wings and hallways and doors. It is easy to lose your way, and convoluted to get where youíre going. Regulus is glad that Remus seems to have some idea because his brain feels too fuzzy to problem solve at the moment, the four of them riding the elevator up several floors before taking multiple turns that get them to one of the many reception areas. Theyíve barely gotten up to the desk when a voice cuts through the space. ØBoys!Æ Marlene comes flying out of her seat, arms instantly wrapping around Remusís neck, then Siriusís, hand flying out to grab hold of Peter as well, who stumbles into the awkward group hug. Sirius attempts to glare at him too but doesnít quite manage it, fondness creeping in. Regulus sits quietly next to them, listening to them talk, legs bouncing up and down, hands clasped between his knees as he watches the door behind the desk. Needs to touch him. To see his eyes. And then maybe itíll stop feeling like there is a two-hundred pound boulder sitting on his chest. Time passes. ØIt hurts me more than I can possibly tell you.Æ ØMom.Æ ØCall me when youíve decided to be less selfish.Æ ØMom.Æ ØGoodbye Regulus.Æ And thatís it. Sheís gone. Regulus looks down at the phone in his hand, the empty screen. He will not cry about this. He wonít. He knew it was coming. Itís the ØI love youÆ thatís really fucked him up. He thinks if she hadnít said that, maybe he wouldnít feel this like a hole in his chest. ØHey,Æ Sirius gets out of his chair almost as soon as Regulus walks back into the waiting area, coming over to him, a small distance between them and the rest of the room. ØYou okay?Æ He isnít sure if Sirius is asking that because something on his face is giving him away or because Sirius was able to make the connection between Regulus getting a random phone call and their mother. He nods at first, not quite trusting himself to speak. And then: ØYeah.Æ Itís pathetic that he cares about this, pathetic that it affects him at all. Especially right now. ØJustèmum, yíknow? Told her I wasnít coming back this summer.Æ He tries to shrug, keep it casual. ØAh,Æ Sirius says. ØIt went about as well as you would expect.Æ Thereís something sad about Siriusís eyes and Regulus has to look away. ØYou want to talk about it?Æ No. Never. He wants to crawl inside his skin like a shell and never be seen again. ØMaybe later,Æ is what he says. Sirius nods, like he understands. Maybe he does. Then he reaches over, pulling Regulus into his side and kissing the top of his head. ØOkay,Æ he says softly. And for whatever reason, it makes it a bit easier to breathe. He can feel people come into the room behind them, but he doesnít really pay any attention to them untilî ØAlice!Æ Marlene and Lily are both on their feet, the three of them running into one another with a force that nearly sends them all toppling over onto the linoleum floor. Next to them stands the older boy from the gameîFrank. He looks mildly amused as he stares at the three girls wobbling about. ØSorry it took so long,Æ the new girlîAliceîsays, a little breathless as they finally let go of one another. ØTook a while to get everyone patched up. Those boys really took a beating.Æ ØHell yeah they did,Æ Sirius cheers from across the room, earning him sharp looks and a lot of shushing from the nurses. ØNot sure itís something I would be proud of butîØ Alice abruptly cuts off as her eyes fall on one of the other faces sitting in the waiting room. ØOh my god, Barty?Æ If Barty is surprised to see her he doesnít show it, but then, expressions donít really seem to be his strong suit. ØHi.Æ ØI was looking for you back at the arena,Æ she says, stepping forward. ØI wanted to make sure you were alright.Æ Her eyes seem to do a quick scan of him now. Thereís a faint bruise on his chin, his knuckles shredded, but otherwise he looks better than most of them. ØDid someone look at you at least?Æ ØIím fine,Æ he says, ignoring her question. ØJust bruises.Æ She laughs a little, shaking her head. ØChrist, well, youíre in better shape than Lestrange, thatís for sure.Æ ØHeís lucky the cops came to his rescue.Æ ØYeah, lets maybe not say that bit out loud,Æ Evan murmurs beside him, though he, for some reason Regulus cannot fathom, seems charmed by Bartyís clearly violent tendencies. ØWait,Æ Aliceís eyes bounce between Evan and Barty. ØIs this why youíre here?Æ to Barty. And then, without waiting for a reply. ØAre you Rosier?Æ Evan seems slightly surprised to be recognized, ØErîyeah?Æ Alice is looking at Barty again. Smiling. ØYour defenceman with the beautiful hands?Æ Now Evan is looking at Barty too, brow arched, smug smile already beginning to form in the corner of his mouth. ØYou talk about my hands?Æ Barty rolls his eyes, though his cheeks have started to flush, which might be the most emotion Regulus has ever seen him express. ØI talk about hockey.Æ ØUh-huh.Æ ØI do.Æ ØSure,Æ like heís gonna let it go. And then: ØMy hockey.Æ ØWhatever.Æ ØI think you have beautiful hands too.Æ ØIím going to leave.Æ ØSure you are.Æ Sirius leans into Regulusís side, whispering: ØAre theyè?Æ Regulus shakes his head, looking at Cerci whoís watching the boys in question with charmed amusement. ØNo idea.Æ More waiting. He supposes this room is aptly named. Lily is currently asleep, head on Maryís shoulder. Frank apparently had the foresight to bring a deck of cards, so him, Alice, Dorcas, Marlene and Peter are all playing some kind of poker game. Cerci and Evan are chatting, itís a conversation that seems to require minimal input from Barty, though despite this, he never takes his eyes off of them. Never seems to lose interest or get bored, like heís trying to soak them up. Sirius and Regulus are leaning against the wall. Not talking. Just waiting. Remus will occasionally look up from the book heís reading, eyeing Sirius like heís looking for damage. Getting ready to run to his side the minute Sirius needs him. Regulus feels like heís almost fallen into a trance. The anxiety and adrenaline of the first few hours wearing off, leaving him justèfloating. The world around him blurry. Itís because of this, that when Euphemia Potter finally walks through the doors heís been staring at nearly all night, he doesnít even blink. Brain on autopilot. At least, until Sirius steps forward. They go right to one another, Euphemia wrapping her arms around him, whispering something in his ear that Regulus canít hear, Sirius nods, eyes a little glassy. If you didnít know any better youíd think she was his mother. Then again, maybe youíd think that even if you did know better. Eventually, they pull back, everyone else on their feet, card games and small talk forgotten. Peter and Remus both step forward, each getting their own embrace from Euphemia. She looks out at the rest of them, one arm still around Sirius. ØThank you all for being here,Æ she looks tired, but she still smiles like she means it. Reminding Regulus painfully of James. Both of them so bright. ØIím sorry itís taken me so long to come out, the test result were a bit slow. James is fine, he has a broken clavicle, sprained neck, and quite a severe concussion, but heíll make a full recovery.Æ There are some relieved faces around the room, though personally, Regulus feels the return of his nausea. The idea of any part of James being broken unbearable to him. ØUnfortunately, because of the concussion, and also because itís so late, theyíve told me he canít have any visitors right now. But he should be able to see you tomorrow after eleven am. Iím so sorry, I know youíve all been waiting for so long but please know we appreciate your love and support so, so much.Æ Nobody is particularly upset by this news, Regulus imagines itís what they were expecting. It must be nearing midnight at this point. They just wanted to know that James would be okay. People start packing up, but before anyone really moves, Euphemia says something to Sirius who nods, looking at Regulus and waving him over. Stupidly, Regulus points at his chest, mouthing Me? as though thereís anyone else it could be. Appropriately, Sirius rolls his eyes, waving again. A bit uncertainly, Regulus walks towards the small group surrounding Euphemia Potter. She smiles at him, when he reaches her. ØHi hun, itís good to see you again.Æ ØUhîYou too Mrs. Potter.Æ ØEffie.Æ ØRight.Æ She smiles again, before: ØI was wondering if you would be willing to come back with me?Æ Regulus blinks. ØIîbutîto see James?Æ he finally manages to stutter. She nods, which only furthers Regulusís confusion. ØYou said he wasnít well enough for visitors?Æ he isnít sure why heís fighting this. Like he hasnít been standing here dying to see James for the past few hours. ØHe isnít well enough, strictly speaking, but, because of the concussion heís a bitèconfused? And he also doesnít have much short term memory at the moment.Æ Regulus feels his stomach lurch. ØHeís lost his memory?Æ he asks, horrified. Euphemia reaches out, taking his shoulder, grounding him. ØShort term memory, and only temporarily. The doctors expect it to pass sometime in the next twelve hours. In the mean time, however, he seems to think that youíre the one whoís been admitted to the hospital and heís been quite worried about you. Hasnít stopped asking to see you since he woke up.Æ Shame washes over Regulus, because it is not hard to imagine why Jamesís brain would be making that specific connection. It makes him feel like some fundamental layer of his protection has been peeled back. ØOh,Æ is the only thing that comes out of his mouth. ØThe nurses say it would better if we could get him to calm down a bit and Iím not sure weíll manage that if he canít see you.Æ I just want to see him. See him. See him Oh James. ØBut of course,Æ she goes on. ØIf youíre too tiredîØ ØWhat?Æ he interrupts, waking up. ØNo, no of course not. Of course IíllîIíd like to see him, please.Æ She smiles softly. ØWonderful,Æ with that she turns back to Peter, Remus and Sirius. ØIíll see you boys in the morning then?Æ ØFirst thing,Æ Sirius says, without hesitating. Regulus looks at him. ØWait, youíre notè?Æ Sirius shakes his head. ØTechnically, theyíre not even supposed to be letting you see him,Æ and Regulus isnít sure if the guilt is that visible on his face or if Sirius just knows him well enough, but he reaches out and squeezes his arm. ØItís alright Reg, okay?Æ he isnít sure either of them quite believe that, but he appreciates that Sirius is trying. ØJustètake care of him, yeah?Æ Regulus swallows with difficulty. ØYeah.Æ They all hug goodbye, Sirius insisting Regulus show him his phone so that he can ensure it is on and fully charged. And then, suddenly, he is being led away by Euphemia Potter. His heart pounding about a mile a minute. He has no idea what Jamesís parents know or think about him. What James said over Christmas, or what heís said since having his head smashed into the ice. He keeps sneaking glances at Euphemia out of the corner of his eye, like heíll find the answers to his questions written across her face. ØItís just here,Æ she says, nodding down a short hallway. ØA warning, heíll probably forget everything you say after a few minutes, itís a bit disconcerting, so just try to prepare yourself for it.Æ Regulus feels his stomach twist, but he nods his head anyway. ØRight. Okay.Æ The door to the room is open, itís small, the only windows facing the hallway instead of the outside world, the bed pressed to the wall, chairs on either side of it. Fleamont Potter is in one, looking tense as he watches his son. James is sat up in bed, agitated expression on his face. His right arm is in a sling, held tight against his chest, a bandage on his head, IV in his free arm. His face is a bit scratched up. His uniform stripped off of him, blue hospital gown in its place. Regulus feels himself stumble across the threshold. Stupidly, he feels like crying. But then Jamesís voice cuts through the room. ØRegulus!Æ ØErîØ he gives Jamesís parents a sidelong look, Euphemia now standing at her husbandís side, hand on his shoulder. ØYeah. Hi.Æ ØAre you okay? What happened?Æ ØUhîØ ØYou were with the doctor,Æ James rambles on before Regulus can answer. ØYou were with the doctor and thenîand thenîØ his face scrunches up, eyes creasing in concentration. ØAnd then I was here and they wouldnít let me see you andîwhat happened?Æ Regulus opens and closes his mouth but he isnít sure what to say. He knows what Jamesís mom told him about James being confused, about him asking for Regulus. But thereís something about experiencing it in real time that is disorienting on a whole other level. ØWe told you sweetheart,Æ Euphemia chimes in when Regulus canít seem to find his words. ØYou were hurt during your game.Æ James looks over at her, face continuing to scrunch. ØMom? What are you doing here?Æ Regulus thinks he hears Fleamont make a wounded noise but his wife only smiles kindly. ØWe were there, at the arena, watching.Æ ØOh,Æ James says, though it doesnít sound like that makes much sense to him at all. Itís odd, like heís seeing them without really taking in what it means that theyíre there. Listening to what theyíre saying without really absorbing the words. James turns back to Regulus. ØBut youíre okay?Æ Regulus swallows, throat tight as he forces himself to speak. ØYeah. Yes. Iím okay.Æ ØReally?Æ ØYes.Æ ØAre you lying to me again?Æ Regulusís cheeks go pink, and he canít look at Jamesís parents. Canít imagine what theyíre thinking. ØNo. Iím not. I promise.Æ ØPromise,Æ James repeats, and then, after a moment, nods his head. Satisfied. ØOkay. Can we go now?Æ ØUhîin a bit. We need to rest first.Æ James nods again, as though that makes perfect sense, before sitting back against his pillows, eyes getting droopy. Regulus isnít sure what to doîwhether he should stand closer to the bed, or take the other available chair? Except Euphemia is still standing and he canít possibly take a seat away from Jamesís mother. He fidgets in place, weight passing back and forth between his feet. Maybe heíll just sit on the floor? ØRegulus!Æ Jamesís voice shocks him out of his thoughts, James suddenly sitting bolt upright in bed. ØWhereís Regulus?Æ eyes wide and frantic. ØWhereísîØ ØHey, itís okay,Æ he finally forces himself to close the rest of the space between him and the bed. ØHey James, look, look, Iím right here.Æ James does look at him, and it takes a moment, like he canít recognize him right away. But then: ØRegulus! Are you okay? What happened?Æ He does his best not to let it show how much this unnerves him. Having the same conversation they just had. ØIîIím fine. You got hurt, during the game.Æ James just blinks at him, and then, shaking his head slightly. ØBut youíre okay?Æ ØYeah, yes. Iím okay.Æ ØYou promise?Æ Regulus swallows, reaching out to grab Jamesís good hand, giving it a squeeze. ØI promise.Æ ØOkay,Æ James says. ØOkay. I was worried.Æ And Regulus isnít sure why thatís what it takes for him to really understand whatís happening here. That in a moment of fear and panic this is the memory Jamesís brain latched onto. The one itís replaying over and over again. How scared James must have been, that night at the hospital. And the day after. And maybe the weeks since. How much it affected him. Regulus knows he hurts himself. And most of the time he figures thatís okay really, because he should be allowed to. Because he deserves it. But he sometimes forgets, how his self-hatred can turn the people around him into collateral damage. ØIím sorry,Æ he finally manages, voice weak. ØIím sorry you were worried.Æ ØSíokay,Æ James says, squeezing his hand back as he leans against his pillows again. ØIím just going to close my eyes for a bit and then we can go.Æ Regulus nods, the motion a little jerky. ØOkay.Æ It happens over and over again. James waking up. Afraid. Wondering where Regulus is. Making him promise heís alright. His mom tells Regulus that it was worse before. That when he wasnít here they had a harder time calming James down. That he would spiral. Wondering where Regulus was, not able to understand anyone when they told him. In the end Regulus doesnít sit in a chair or on the floor, but on the side of the bed, Jamesís hand clutched in his. He makes sure James can see him as soon as he wakes up, gets better at answering his questions. Euphemia tells him he can take a break for a bit, take one of the chairs, try to sleep, but he shakes his head. He doesnít want James to be afraid. Not for a second. Not if he can help it. James starts to close his eyes for longer periods. Of course, never too long. Every two hours a nurse comes in to check on him, wake him up, look him over. They donít make Regulus move, instead smiling kindly at him as they perform their assessments. Regulus isnít sure what they think, tries not to worry about it. He still does worry but, not enough to leave Jamesís side. To let go of his hand. He doesnít have any real sense of time, or how much of it has past, until Euphemia comes over and taps him gently on the shoulder. ØHey honey,Æ she says softly. ØYour brother just texted me, him and the boys are on their way. Weíre just going to go grab some breakfast,Æ she nods to her husband who hasnít said a word this whole time. At least not as far as Regulus can tell. ØDo you want anything?Æ He blinks, eyes coming back to her. ØNo, Iím okay thanks.Æ She reaches out, squeezing his shoulder. ØWeíll be right back.Æ He nods, listening to their steps on the linoleum. If Sirius is on his way it must be morning. He yawns, head feeling heavy, skin dry and tight. Thereís a stale film lying on top of his skin, his teeth, he can just feel it. He needs a shower and fresh clothes. Sighing, he looks down at where heís rubbing his thumb across the back of Jamesís hand. Jamesís hands are surprisingly soft, and warm, and Regulus finds himself daydreaming about building a home right in his palm. About living between his fingers. ØRegulus?Æ He looks up as Jamesís eyes blink open. ØIím fine,Æ Regulus says instantly, before James can get worked up. ØYouíre here because you got injured at the game, but Iím fine, I promise.Æ James just stares at him for a moment, before: ØDid we win?Æ Regulus blinks. Thatís a new one. ØDid youîdo you remember?Æ Jamesís brows draw together, looking at Regulus like heís the one with brain damage. ØFrozen Four, finals, we were playing Salazar, I scored a hatty. Did we win?Æ A laugh bubbles out of Regulusís mouth, but about half-way through it starts to get all cut up and choked, Regulus bringing his hand to his face as, embarrassingly, he starts to cry. ØWoah,Æ James tries to lean forward but Regulus only shakes his head, hands still covering his mouth, tears streaming down his cheeks. ØHey, hey, what is it? Whatís wrong?Æ Itís ridiculous how many times James has asked him that over the past few hours. How James has sat here with his head cracked open and his body broken and tried to take care of Regulus. Regulus who hasnít even been able to work up the courage to face him in weeks. He wants to hug James, to hold him, but heís worried about his bones. His head. So instead Regulus takes the hand heíd been holding and brings it to his mouth, kissing the palm, the wrist, the knuckles. Heís not sure if heís allowed to, but the other boy doesnít pull away. ØRegulus?Æ James asks softly. He shakes his head again, clutching Jamesís hand between both of his. ØYour head hit the boards. I watched. Youîyou dinít get up. You didnítîthey had to carry you off.Æ James makes an ØahÆ shape with his mouth. ØThat explains why it currently feels like a semi is driving back and forth over my skull.Æ Regulus lets out something choked that might be a laugh. ØIt was really fucking scary,Æ he whispers. ØIím fine, I promise.Æ But Regulus shakes his head. ØYouíre not,Æ he sniffles. ØYour brain is bruised and your bones are broken and youíre in the hospital andîØ ØHey, breathe, just breathe, okay?Æ But Regulus is still shaking his head, clutching desperately at Jamesís hand. ØIím sorry that I lied to you,Æ something complicated flickers across Jamesís face, and this is almost certainly not the time or place for this conversation, but Regulus canít help it, the words already pouring out of him. ØIím sorry that I hid things. Iím sorry I made you hide things. Iím sorry that I havenít called. I didnít know how. I didnít want you to feel like you were trapped.Æ ØTrapped?Æ James repeats, confused. ØWhat are you talking about?Æ ØLike you were stuck with me, because Iímîfuck, I donít knowîbecause Iím sick and you canít just dump someone when theyíre sick. And I didnítîI told you ages ago, that eventually you were going to figure out that Iím not worth the hassle and I didnít want to have to see it. I didnít want to have to watch you feel bad about it. So I just thoughtîthought Iíd save us both the trouble, you know?Æ James is staring at him. He looks tired, half propped up in bed, justèstaring. And then, eventually: ØMy head is killing me.Æ Regulus instantly feels guilty. ØRight, right of course,Æ he sniffles, rubbing at his face, trying to pull himself together. ØSorry. Iíll go get a nurse orîyour parents will be back soon. SoîØ he tries to get up but now James is the one holding onto him. ØMy head is killing me,Æ he repeats. ØMy whole body, actually, feels a bit like itís just been put through a meat grinder.Æ Regulus grimaces at the image. ØSo, Iím probably not going to say this in the best way, but goddamnit youíre so fucking stupid.Æ Regulus feels his cheeks flush, ØI know. I shouldnít have brought this all up right now, not when youíre just waking up and in the fucking hospital. I donít know whatís wrong with me.Æ ØThatís not what Iím talking about,Æ he sounds weary. ØRegulus, come on.Æ But Regulus isnít really sure what that means, so he just stares back at James helplessly, and eventually the older boy sighs. ØI stood outside your door,Æ something about the way he says it, the way his voice wobbles just a little, the raw look in his bruised eyes. ØI begged you not to push me away. I begged you to let me take care of you.Æ Regulus closes his eyes, breath stuttering. ØI know.Æ ØSo then howîØ ØI didnít believe you,Æ eyes still shut. ØI donít believe you.Æ The truth is easier sometimes, when you canít see it. Canít see what it does to the people you care about. ØAh,Æ James says finally, a crack. A fracture. A bone that wonít set. ØDo you really want me James? Really? Or do you just want to do the right thing? Just want someone to rescue? Because I canít just be your good deed. The fucking baby bird you save from the side of the road. I donít want that. I donít want to always be that to you.Æ Their hands are still clutching at one another, Regulus no longer sure who is holding onto who. His eyes remaining closed. There are noises outside the roomîvoices, footsteps, wheelchairs. Regulus is pretty sure heís shaking. ØRegulus,Æ James says softly, pulling on their joint hands until, eventually, Regulus opens his eyes. ØI donít want to save you, I just want toèlove you,Æ he gives Regulus a sad sort of smile, eyes a little damp. ØThatís it. Thatís all. Really.Æ Oh. Well. If thatís all. Regulus doesnít know how to respond exactly, so for some reason the first words that come out of his mouth are: ØThatís the second time youíve said you love me.Æ Jamesís smile is maybe a bit sturdier. ØI think it a lotÆ ØFuck off,Æ Regulus chokes out, looking away. ØSorry.Æ ØYouíre soîØ ØWhat?Æ ØIîØ ØTell me.Æ He shakes his head, almost certainly crying again. Finally, after several pointless seconds of trying to regain some kind of composure, he allows his eyes to return to Jamesís face. ØI love you too.Æ James gets too bright then. Like this is a gift. It isnít. Regulus is not a prize. Not deserving of this kind of joy. But he doesnít take it back. Doesnít fight it. And James just keeps looking at him like heís hung the moon. ØKiss me,Æ the older boy says finally. Regulus scoffs, face flushing. ØYouíre injured.Æ ØMy mouth isnít.Æ ØYour parents could walk in.Æ But James doesnít back down. ØKiss me.Æ And, well, heís never been much good at saying no to James Potter. Not really. Itís a chaste thing, the innocent press of lips. Still, it makes something flutter in Regulusís stomach, and tingle in his fingertips. When he pulls back he rests his forehead gently against Jamesís, catching his breath. ØRegulus?Æ James asks quietly, and Regulus hums in response. ØBe my boyfriend.Æ Regulus blinks, pulling back slightly. ØWhat?Æ ØBe my boyfriend. Be with me. Properly. No more hiding, no more secrets, Iím tired of it. Be with me, okay? Is thatècan you let us have that?Æ Let us have that. He wants to tell James that heíll regret this. Because itíll make his future more complicated. His life harder. But Regulus has been giving James Potter outs all year. And every time heís just dug his heels in deeper. So maybe James is a masochist. Hell, maybe Regulus is too. ØOkay,Æ though his voice shakes. ØYeah?Æ Regulus tries to breathe through all the fears that are bubbling up to the surface. Threatening to suffocate him. ØYes,Æ he manages eventually. ØYes, we can have this.Æ And then James is kissing him again. ØWell, this is new.Æ Regulus instantly rips himself away, sitting up to find Jamesís parents walking back into the room. His mom appearing as amused as his father is uncomfortable. Regulus tries to get off the bed, put more distance between them, but James doesnít let him, holding fiercely to his hand as he looks right at his parents. ØI think my headís working a bit better now,Æ he says, casual, like his parents didnít just walk in on them making out. ØIím glad to hear it,Æ his mother is holding a white paper coffee cup between her hands, and she comes forward, leaning down to kiss his forehead. ØHow are you feeling?Æ ØLike I just got hit by a train.Æ She gives him a small smile. ØYes, I think thatís to be expected. If you could do your best not to get injured like this again, me and your father would very much appreciate it.Æ He snorts, wincing a little when he does. ØIíll try.Æ ØThank you,Æ she kisses him again before straightening up. ØWe should get the nurse to come in and check you out. Iíll be right back, okay love?Æ ØYeah, okay.Æ She gives Regulus a kind smile before leaving the room. Leaving them alone with Jamesís father, who Regulus still hasnít heard speak. He doesnít break that silence now, looking down at the coffee in his hands, standing awkwardly where his wife left him. ØDad?Æ James says finally. It takes a second, but eventually the older man looks up. His eyes are a bit damp, and when he speaks his words are shaking all over the place. ØYou really scared us there kid.Æ ØIím sorry.Æ He huffs, shaking his head and rubbing his free hand under his glasses. ØIím justîØ he starts, before letting out a breath that sounds like itís been held in for a long time. ØIím just glad youíre okay.Æ James tries to smile, itís a wobbly thing. ØYeah, me too.Æ Thereís another pause, and Regulus can feel it, feel the moment Jamesís fatherís eyes find their hands, tangled together in the blankets, his expression unreadable. Regulus has to fight the urge to pull away again, to throw himself across the room, because James is still clutching him. Because he promised him no more hiding. ØDad? This is Regulus,Æ James says finally. His father looks up from their hands, giving James a wry smile. ØYes, weíve met.Æ But James doesnít falter. ØHeís my boyfriend.Æ And despite the fact that his parents just walked in on them kissing, that sentence still makes the air hitch in Regulusís chest. James and his father stare at one another for a long time, and Regulus can tell there is a weight to this moment he canít possibly understand. Layers buried too deep for him to see. It feels like ages before Jamesís father finally nods, eyes flicking back to Regulus. And if his smile is a little awkward, itís no less sincere. ØNice to meet you, Regulus-Jamesís-boyfriend.Æ Severus ØCome in!Æ Luciusís voice calls through his office door. Severus pauses for only a second before pushing on. The older man is sat at his desk, blond hair in a ponytail, eyes on the computer screen in front of him. ØJust give me one second,Æ he says, without looking up. Severus stands in the middle of the office, hand gripping the strap of his messenger bag. Waiting. He doesnít feel nervous, though the sweat soaking his one and only dress shirt might suggest otherwise. Heís just impatient. Just wants this over with. ØAnddddd there we are,Æ Lucius clicks something emphatically on his keyboard before swivelling his chair and facing Severus properly. ØCleaned out your desk then?Æ ØYes sir.Æ ØGood man, youíre faster than the others. I like that about you, very efficient. Youíve been an excellent intern this year Severus, truly, top marks. If youíre ever in need of a reference feel free to put my name down.Æ He smiles with his too white teeth. Severusís grip on his bag tightens, and he feels the need to inhale before he speaks. ØThank you. But that wonít be necessary.Æ Luciusís brow creases. ØOh? Are you changing fields or something? Not switching over to the arts are you,Æ he laughs. Itís an empty noise. ØNo sir, still sticking with business.Æ ØWell then,Æ Lucius opens his arms. ØSurely I can help you out? One of the bonuses of being deaf is it is much easier to ignore people yelling at you. But nothing really happens. At least, not at first. Well, if thatís what ___ want, his mother says finally, shooting his father nervous looks. Remus nods, despite the anxious buzzing in his stomach, he is sure. It is. His father is just staring at him, Remus half expects him to get up and just walk out. But eventually, he speaks: Why? Remus blinks. What? Why? I donít understand. What __ _ missing? Why have you been fighting me on this? Itís completely irrational Remus. Remus had been prepared for another shouting match, ready for the old familiar back and forth. But this? This feels like something different. Like his father genuinely wants an answer. Why have you been fighting me? he asks finally. Throwing his father's question back at him. Lyall's face scrunches up. Youíre my kid, he says finally. I want you __ have the best life possible. I want __ be able __ give you the best life possible. I couldnít before, and Iím sorry about that, we didnít have the money. I canít imagine how hard __ was for you, growing up. But now I can do something about it. And I donít understand why you wonít let me help you? Itís funny, if he tilts his head just right, he thinks he can see it through his fatherís eyes. Putting him in regular school, in hockey, being so intense about his speech therapy, not wanting him to have a translator. Those were gifts, as far as his father was concerned, he was bending over backwards to make sure Remus was as normal as possible. Not because he was embarrassed. Not because he was punishing Remus for being somehow lesser than the son he had imagined. But because the only life his father could understand as happy, as fulfilled, was one that looked just like his. And he had tried to give Remus that. Never understanding. How much he hurt him in the process. I told you at Christmas, his father says finally, when Remus remains still for too long. I worry about you, about what will happen when you get older. When you leave school. Iím trying to protect you here. Iím trying to set you up for success. Dad, he manages finally. I need you to listen to me, alright? His father looks at him for a moment before nodding his head warily,Alright. I promise you I understand how much this means, I understand how much this operation has helped some people. Maybe the implant would work, maybe it wouldnít. But it's not really worth the risk for me, because Iím not looking for something to fix this. I'm not saying it's never hard, I'm just saying it's not only hard. I like signing and I've started to find communities, with other people who are deaf. He sees it in the faces of James and Sirius too, the mildly star struck expression. They all thought he was going to be the NHLís next darling, and now all of a sudden heís sitting with them in their shitty college bar. No one says anything of course. Including Barty, who, more often than not, seems content to simply sit next to Evan. Or be on the receiving end of Cerciís smiles. ØOh my god!Æ Sirius drops his near empty glass of beer onto the table, which, thankfully, doesnít shatter. ØItís my song. Theyíre playing my song.Æ He gets to his feet, pulling Remus along with him. ØJames!Æ he shouts over his shoulder. Øget your ass out here. The rest of you too!Æ Thereís barely anyone on the dance floor, and this certainly isnít the song that would make most people get out of their seats, but then, Sirius isnít most people. ØCímon,Æ James presses in close, whispering in Regulusís ear, hot breath sneaking down his neck. ØDance with me.Æ Regulus means to say no. Means to laugh in his face. But James is so warm. His mouth at Regulusís ear. Hands at his hips. That somehow, without his permission, he finds himself standing, finds himself being led out to the middle of the room. Sirius and Remus are standing next to the speakers, Sirius signing. Dorcas and Marlene, Mary and Lily, Peter, Cerci, Evanîtheyíre all on their feet. Shouting the words at one another. James grabs hold of Regulus with his good hand and spins him around. The pair stumbling together. James kisses him then. And Regulus is kissing him back. And somewhere along the way kissing turns into singing. James whispering the words into Regulusís mouth. Can't we give ourselves one more chance? Why can't we give love that one more chance? Why can't we give love, give love, give love, give love, give love, give love, give love, give love, give love? And for a moment. Just a moment. Regulus Black thinks maybe heís okay. Maybe, as insane as it sounds, heís even happy. This is our last dance This is our last dance This is ourselves Under pressure Chapter End Notes I WANTED TO DO MORE EDITING BUT UNFORTUNATELY I REALLY DO HAVE A REAL LIFE WITH REAL OBLIGATIONS THAT I HAVE BEEN IGNORING FOR HOURS AND CANNOT CONTINUE TO IGNORE SOOOOOOOOOO I"M SORRY I HOPE IT WAS OKAY i really liked getting to parallel the first chapter, starting with Regulus in therapy and ending with them all singing together at the bar ANYWAY hopefully this ending gave you everything you were looking for and has you feeling all warm and fuzzy :) MERRY HALLOWS EVE <3 Works inspired by this one Kill Your Darlings (Podfic) by illustriousday Please drop by the Archive and comment to let the creator know if you enjoyed their work!
